messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no routing command do?
The no routing command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no routing.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no routing command?
Syntax: no routing
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx command do?
The vsx command creates the VSX context on the switch. The no form of this command disables the VSX context on the switch and removes all related configuration settings. Syntax: no vsx.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx command?
Syntax: no vsx
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx active-forwarding command do?

The vsx active-forwarding command configures VSX active-forwarding on an interface VLAN. The no form of this command unconfigures VSX active-forwarding on a VLAN interface. Syntax: no vsx active-forwarding.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx active-forwarding command?
Syntax: no vsx active-forwarding
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command enables VSX synchronization for the following for a logical interface or a LAG instance: • Access lists • IRDP configurations • QoS • Rate limits • Port filter configurations • VLAN associations This command enables VSX synchronization for individual associations and to the combination of associations to the interface context. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface on the peer switch. IMPORTANT: When enabling VSX synchronization under a physical interface, under a VLAN interface, or a VSX LAG, create on the secondary switch the physical interface, VLAN interface, or VSX LAG with the same name and routing setting as on the primary switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command enables VSX sync of active gateways or policies associated under an interface. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface vlan on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync aaa command do?

The vsx-sync aaa command enables VSX synchronization of all AAA configurations, including user, RADIUS server, and TACACS+ server, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize AAA configurations associated with a particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global AAA configurations, but it does not remove the existing global AAA feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync aaa.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync aaa command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync aaa
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync acl-log-timer command do?

The vsx-sync acl-log-timer command enables VSX synchronization of access list log timer configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. Chapter 116 VSX commands 1741 The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of access list log timer configurations to the secondary peer. However, it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync acl-log-timer.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync acl-log-timer command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync acl-log-timer
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync arp-security command do?

The vsx-sync arp-security command enables VSX synchronization of the ARP security configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. After you enter vsx-sync arp-security, you must enter vsx-sync mclag-interfaces for enabling VSX synchronization for the ARP security feature. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of ARP security configurations on VLAN mode and LAG interface mode to the secondary peer switch. However, it does not remove the existing ARP security configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync arp-security.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync arp-security command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync arp-security
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync bfd-global command do?

The vsx-sync bfd-global command enables syncing of global BFD configurations, such as echo-src-ip-address, detect-multiplier, min- transmit-interval, and min-receive-interval, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. IMPORTANT: This command enables VSX synchronization only at the top level and not at the context level. The no form of this command disables the syncing of global BFD configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing global BFD feature configurations from it. Syntax: no vsx-sync bfd-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync bfd-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync bfd-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync bgp command do?

The vsx-sync bgp command enables syncing of BGP configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. Chapter 116 VSX commands 1743 The no form of this command disables syncing BGP, as path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync bgp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync bgp command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync bgp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync copp-policy command do?

The vsx-sync copp-policy command enables VSX synchronization of CoPP policy configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global CoPP configurations, but it does not remove the existing global CoPP configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync copp-policy.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync copp-policy command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync copp-policy
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dcb-global command do?

The vsx-sync dcb-global command enables VSX synchronization of global DCBx configurations from the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of global DCBx configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DCBx feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dcb-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dcb-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync dcb-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dhcp-relay command do?

The vsx-sync dhcp-relay command enables VSX synchronization of DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 relay configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of the command disables the VSX synchronization of DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 relay configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 relay configurations from the secondary VSX peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-relay.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dhcp-relay command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-relay
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dhcp-server command do?

The vsx-sync dhcp-server command enables VSX synchronization of all DHCPv4 server configurations, including external storage configurations, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize DHCPv4 server configurations associated with a particular VRF, configure the same VRF on the peer device. Only the primary VSX node answers DHCP service requests, and leases can only be exported from the primary VSX node. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of DHCPv4 server configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DHCPv4 server feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-server.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dhcp-server command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-server
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dhcpv6-server command do?

The vsx-sync dhcpv6-server command enables VSX synchronization of all DHCPv6 server configurations, including external storage configurations, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize DHCPv6 server configurations associated with a particular VRF, configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of DHCPv6 server configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DHCPv6 server feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcpv6-server.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dhcpv6-server command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcpv6-server
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dns command do?

The vsx-sync dns command enables VSX synchronization of the global DNS configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize DNS configurations associated with particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for global DNS configurations, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync dns.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dns command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync dns
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync evpn command do?

The vsx-sync evpn command enables syncing of all EVPN context-related configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command disables syncing EVPN configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE: As a prerequisite, VLAN vsx-sync must be enabled separately for the VLAN configurations inside EVPN context to get synced. Chapter 116 VSX commands 1749. Syntax: no vsx-sync evpn.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync evpn command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync evpn
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync icmp-tcp command do?

The vsx-sync icmp-tcp command enables VSX synchronization of IP ICMP configurations, including ip icmp unreachable, ip icmp redirect, and ip icmp throttle configurations, on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize ip icmp unreachable, ip icmp redirect, and ip icmp throttle configurations, associated with particular VRF, configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of the command disables the VSX synchronization of IP ICMP configurations to the secondary peer. However, it does not remove the existing IP ICMP configurations from the secondary VSX peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync icmp-tcp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync icmp-tcp command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync icmp-tcp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync keychain command do?

The vsx-sync keychain command enables synchronizing of key chain configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration synchronization from secondary to primary peer. If any additional modification or configuration is made on the primary for the key chain set of features, the features will be auto-synchronized. The no form of the command disables synchronizing key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synchronized configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync keychain.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync keychain command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync keychain
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync lldp command do?

The vsx-sync lldp command enables VSX synchronization of the LLDP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of this command disable VSX synchronization of LLDP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing LLDP feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Chapter 116 VSX commands 1751. Syntax: no vsx-sync lldp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync lldp command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync lldp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync loop-protect-global command do?

The vsx-sync loop-protect-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global loop protect configurations, such as transmit-interval and re- enable-timer, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global loop protect configurations, but it does not remove the existing global loop protect feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync loop-protect-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync loop-protect-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync loop-protect-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync mac-lockout command do?

The vsx-sync mac-lockout command enables VSX synchronization of the MAC Lockout configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of this command disables syncing MAC Lockout configurations to the secondary peer. However, it does not remove the existing MAC Lockout feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync mac-lockout.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync mac-lockout command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync mac-lockout
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command do?

The vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command enables the VSX synchronization of VSX LAG interface associations and attributes on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The Usage section in this topic provides a listing of specific associations and attributes that are synchronized to the secondary switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global VSX LAG and attributes, but it does not remove the existing VSX LAG feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync mclag-interfaces.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync mclag-interfaces
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync nd-snooping command do?

The vsx-sync nd-snooping command enables VSX synchronization of ND snooping configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize ND snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing ND snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync nd-snooping.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync nd-snooping command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync nd-snooping
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync neighbor command do?

The vsx-sync neighbor command enables VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. If any new modification or additional configuration is made on the primary node for IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration, they will be auto-synced. The no form of this command VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync neighbor command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync ospf command do?

The vsx-sync ospf command enables syncing of OSPF (including OSPFv2 and OPSFv3), route map, and key chain configurations on the primary VSX switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize OSPF configurations at the port level context, configure the same port on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing of OSPF, route map, and key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE: The OSPF router ID is not synchronized. This exclusion is needed because the router ID uniquely identifies the router. The two OSPF routers with the same router ID do not form an adjacency between them. Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync ospf command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync policy-global command do?

The vsx-sync policy-global command enables VSX synchronization of global classifier policy configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command disables VSX synchronization of global policy configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync policy-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync policy-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync policy-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync qos-global command do?

The vsx-sync qos-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, such as CoS map, DSCP map, and trust policy, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync qos command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, but it does not remove the existing global QoS feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync qos-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync qos-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync qos-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync route-map command do?

The vsx-sync route-map command enables syncing of all As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from the secondary to primary peer. The no form of this command disables syncing of As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. 1758 AOS-CX 10.06 Command-Line Interface Guide. Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync route-map command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync sflow command do?

The vsx-sync sflow command enables VSX synchronization of the sFlow configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global sFlow configurations, but it does not remove the existing global sFlow feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync sflow.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync sflow command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync sflow
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync sflow-global command do?

The vsx-sync sflow-global command enables VSX synchronization of the sFlow global configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize sFlow configurations associated with a particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of this command disables VSX synchronization of global sFlow configurations, but it does not remove the existing sFlow feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync sflow-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync sflow-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync sflow-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync snmp command do?

The vsx-sync snmp command enables VSX synchronization of SNMP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize SNMP configurations associated with a particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global SNMP configurations, but it does not remove the existing global SNMP feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync snmp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync snmp command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync snmp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync ssh command do?

The vsx-sync ssh command enables VSX synchronization of SSH server configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize SSH configurations associated with particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global SSH configurations, but it does not remove the existing global SSH feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync ssh.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync ssh command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync ssh
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync static-routes command do?

The vsx-sync static-routes command enables VSX synchronization of static route configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize static route configurations associated with particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global static route configurations, but it does not remove the existing global static route feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync static-routes.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync static-routes command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync static-routes
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync stp-global command do?

The vsx-sync stp-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global STP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. Use the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command to sync context level spanning trees. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global STP configurations, but it does not remove the existing global STP feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync stp-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync stp-global command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync stp-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync time command do?

The vsx-sync time command enables VSX synchronization of time-related configurations, including NTP and time zone configurations, on the primary VSX node on the secondary peer switch. To synchronize NTP configurations associated with a particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global time-related configurations, but it does not remove the existing global time-related feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Chapter 116 VSX commands 1763. Syntax: no vsx-sync time.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync time command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync time
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync udp-forwarder command do?

The vsx-sync udp-forwarder command enables VSX synchronization of UDP forwarder configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of the command disables the VSX synchronization of UDP forwarder configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing udp-forwarder configurations from the secondary VSX peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync udp-forwarder.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync udp-forwarder command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync udp-forwarder
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync vrf command do?

The vsx-sync vrf command enables VSX synchronization of VRF configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize VRF configurations, there is no need to configure the same VRF on the secondary peer. To synchronize VRF configurations associated with a particular L3 interface, no special or extra configuration is required on the primary VSX node. The no form of this command disables syncing VRF configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrf.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync vrf command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync vrf
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. NOTE: BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync vrrp command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show spanning-tree mst-config command do?

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }

Syntax: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]}

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command do?

The default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command configures the default class that is automatically defined for all CoPP policies. The default class cannot be removed, but its configuration can be changed. The default class is applied to traffic that does not match any other class defined for a policy. Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>].

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reset copp-policy { <NAME> | default } command do?

The reset copp-policy { <NAME> | default } command resets an active CoPP policy to match the settings that are currently in effect for the active policy on the switch. Changes made to the active policy that could not be activated are removed from the active policy. When the switch fails to add or modify a class in an active CoPP policy, it is possible the active policy settings on the switch may be out of sync with those defined in the policy. Syntax: reset copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Management of rotated log files?

Management of rotated log files: Rotated log files are compressed and stored locally in /var/log/, regardless of the remote host configuration. Rotated log files are stored with respective time extension to the granularity of hour in the format file1-YYYYMMDDHH.gz (for example, messages-2015080715.gz). Rotated log files are replaced when the number of old rotated log files exceeds three. The newly rotated log file replaces the oldest rotated log file. Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command do?

The logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command specifies the maximum allowed log file size. The no form of this command resets the size of the log file to the default (100 MB). Sets the rotate period for the event logs, stored in the /var/log/event.log file, and authentication logs, stored in the /var/log/auth.log file. Defaults to daily. A log file that exceeds either the logrotate <MAX-SIZE> value or the logrotate period (whichever happens first), triggers rotation for that log file. The no form of this command resets the log rotation period to the default. Syntax: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command?
Syntax: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>

The led locator {on | off | flashing} command sets the state of the locator LED to on, off (default), or flashing. Sets the administrative state of the specified line module. Changes the configuration of the switch to indicate that the specified member and slot number contains, or will contain, a line module. The no form of this command removes the line module and its interfaces from the configuration. If there is a line module installed in the slot, the line module is powered off and then powered on. To view boot-history on the standby, the command must be sent on the standby console. Syntax: show boot-history [all].

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Modify the ACL to allow the ping traffic 2. Unapply the ACL from egress (8400/8320/8325 switches) or Control Plane 3. Ping a destination which is not matched by the ACL. For example, if the ACL is blocking traffic based on. Commands shown in the procedure include: PING 100.1.2.10 (100.1.2.10) 100(128) bytes of data; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the logging command do?

The logging command enables syslog forwarding to a remote syslog server. The no form of this command disables syslog forwarding to a remote syslog server. Syntax: logging {<IPv4-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR> | <HOSTNAME>}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the logging command?
Syntax: logging {<IPv4-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR> | <HOSTNAME>}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the logging persistent-storage command do?

The logging persistent-storage command enables or disables storage of logs in storage. Only logs of the specified severity and above will be preserved in the storage. Syntax: logging persistent-storage [severity {alert|crit|debug|emerg|err|info|notice|warning}].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the logging persistent-storage command?
Syntax: logging persistent-storage [severity {alert|crit|debug|emerg|err|info|notice|warning}]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the diag on-demand command do?
The diag on-demand command runs the diagnostic tests for all modules or for a specified module. Syntax: diag on-demand {fan-tray | line-module | management-module} [<SLOT-ID>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the diag on-demand command?
Syntax: diag on-demand {fan-tray | line-module | management-module} [<SLOT-ID>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show diagnostic events command do?
The show diagnostic events command displays the diagnostic related event logs. Syntax: show diagnostic events.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show diagnostic events command?
Syntax: show diagnostic events
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot command do?
The boot command presents you with the boot menu prompt. You can then specify which boot profile: primary, secondary, or Service OS console. Syntax: boot.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot command?
Syntax: boot
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip command do?

The ip command shows or configures the port with a static IP address (IPv4 only) or enables the DHCP client on the port. An address is set only if a DHCP server is available to provide one. Syntax: ip {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR-NETMASK-GATEWAY>}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip command?
Syntax: ip {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR-NETMASK-GATEWAY>}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ls command do?
The ls command this command lists directory contents. Syntax: ls [<OPTIONS>] [<FILE-NME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ls command?
Syntax: ls [<OPTIONS>] [<FILE-NME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command do?
The md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command this command computes and checks the MD5 message digest. Syntax: md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command?
Syntax: md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command do?

The mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command this command makes directories. This command mounts the SSD partitions to the following locations: /coredump, /logs, /nos, /selftest, and mounts the USB device to /mnt/usb. Users can mount USB flash drives formatted as either FAT16 or FAT32 with a single partition. This command moves (renames) files. Syntax: mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command?
Syntax: mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the pwd command do?
The pwd command displays the current working directory. Syntax: pwd.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the pwd command?
Syntax: pwd
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reboot command do?
The reboot command reboots the Management Module. Syntax: reboot.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the reboot command?
Syntax: reboot
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the rm command do?
The rm command removes files or directories. Syntax: rm [-f | -i | -R | -r] <FILE-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the rm command?
Syntax: rm [-f | -i | -R | -r] <FILE-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the rmdir command do?
The rmdir command removes empty directories. Syntax: rmdir [-p] <DIRECTORY-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the rmdir command?
Syntax: rmdir [-p] <DIRECTORY-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the secure-mode command do?

The secure-mode command sets the secure mode to enhanced or standard secure mode. Also can display the current secure mode. A zeroization is required before switching between enhanced and standard secure modes. The command also displays a message notifying the user that they are already in the targeted secure mode. Syntax: secure-mode <enhanced | standard | status>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the secure-mode command?
Syntax: secure-mode <enhanced | standard | status>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the sh command do?

The sh command launches a bash shell for support purposes. To quit bash, enter exit. This command is not available if enhanced secure mode is set. Unmounts the SSD partitions mounted to the following locations: /coredump, /logs, /nos, /selftest, and unmounts the USB device mounted to /mnt/usb. Verifies and installs a product image. The user can select the primary or secondary boot profile to update and the location of the file. Syntax: sh.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the sh command?
Syntax: sh
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command do?

The tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command the tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command transfers files to and from a remote machine (TFTP a file). Displays the following build strings: • Version. • Build date. • Build time. • Build ID. • SHA. Syntax: tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command?
Syntax: tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is In-System Programming?

In-System Programming: The ISP (In-System Programming) feature provides an automated way to roll out updates to various programmable devices in an AOS-CX network switch, after the product has shipped. ISP is intended to run automatically either at boot time or as new modules are inserted into the chassis at runtime. Show tech command list for the ISP feature Task Displaying versions of all present programmable devices. Displaying stored log files from any ISP updates on the system.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear update-log command do?
The clear update-log command clears stored log files of any In-System Programming updates on the system. Syntax: clear update-log.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear update-log command?
Syntax: clear update-log
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show needed-updates command do?
The show needed-updates command displays whether any programmable devices are in need of an update. Syntax: show needed-updates [next-boot [primary|secondary]].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show needed-updates command?
Syntax: show needed-updates [next-boot [primary|secondary]]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the fastboot command do?
The fastboot command enables fastboot for the system. The no form of this command disables fastboot for the system. Syntax: fastboot.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the fastboot command?
Syntax: fastboot
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show selftest command do?
The show selftest command displays selftest results. Syntax: show selftest [brief] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show selftest command?
Syntax: show selftest [brief] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erase all zeroize command do?

The erase all zeroize command restores the switch to its factory default configuration. You will be prompted before the procedure starts. Once complete, the switch will restart from the primary image with factory default settings. NOTE: Back up all data before running this command as all configuration settings will be lost. Syntax: erase all zeroize.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erase all zeroize command?
Syntax: erase all zeroize
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the terminal-monitor {notify <event|debug|all> | severity <level> | filter keyword} command?
Syntax: terminal-monitor {notify <event|debug|all> | severity <level> | filter keyword}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show terminal-monitor command do?
The show terminal-monitor command shows whether the terminal monitoring is enabled or disabled. Syntax: show terminal-monitor.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show terminal-monitor command?
Syntax: show terminal-monitor

section: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.

task: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about About AOS-CX?

About AOS-CX: AOS-CX introduction AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including: • Automated visibility to help IT organizations scale: The Aruba Network Analytics Engine allows IT to monitor and troubleshoot network, system, application, and security-related issues easily through simple scripts. This engine comes with a built-in time series database that enables customers and developers to create software modules that allow historical troubleshooting, as well as analysis of historical trends to predict and avoid future problems due to scale, security, and performance bottlenecks.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Initial configuration?

Initial configuration: Perform the initial configuration of a factory default switch using one of the following methods: • Load a switch configuration using zero-touch provisioning (ZTP). When ZTP is used, the configuration is loaded from a server automatically when the switch booted from the factory default configuration. • Connect to the switch wirelessly with a mobile device through Bluetooth, and use the Aruba CX Mobile App to deploy an initial configuration from a provided template. The template you choose during the deployment process determines how the management interface is configured. Optionally, as the final deployment step, you can select to import the switch into Net Edit through a WiFI connection to the Net Edit server. Alternatively, you can use the Aruba CX Mobile App to manually configure switch settings and features for a subset of the features you can configure using the CLI. You can also access the CLI through the mobile application.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Bluetooth connections?

To troubleshoot Bluetooth connections, follow the documented procedure: 1. Verify the model number and serial number of the switch to which you are attempting to connect 2. Use the Bluetooth settings on your mobile device to pair and connect the switch to your mobile device.

section: Configuring in-band management on a data port

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure in-band management on a data port?

To configure in-band management on a data port, follow the documented procedure: 1. Use an Ethernet cable to connect a data port to your network 2. Configure a layer 3 interface on the data port 3. Enable SSH support on the interface (on the default VRF) with the command ssh server vrf default 4. Enable the Web UI on the interface (on the default VRF) with the command https-server vrf 5. Log in to the CLI 6. Switch to config context and enable the Web UI on the management port VRF with the command 7. Start your web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar, 8. The Web UI starts and you are prompted to log in. Commands shown in the procedure include: https-server vrf mgmt..

task: Configuring in-band management on a data port

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the management interface?

To configure the management interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the management interface context with the command interface mgmt 2. By default, the management interface on the management port is enabled. If it was disabled, reenable it 3. Use the command ip dhcp to configure the management interface to automatically obtain an address 4. SSH is enabled by default on the management VRF. If disabled, enable SSH with the command ssh.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip static command do?

The ip static command assigns an IPv4 or IPv6 address to the management interface. The no form of this command removes the IP address from the management interface and sets the interface to operate as a DHCP client. Assigns a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 DNS server to the management interface. IPv4 DNS servers can only be configured if a static IPv4 address was assigned to the management interface. IPv6 DNS servers can only be configured if a static IPv6 address was assigned to the management interface. The default gateway should be on the same network segment. The no form of this command removes the DNS servers from the management interface. Syntax: ip static <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip static command?
Syntax: ip static <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface mgmt command do?
The show interface mgmt command shows status and configuration information for the management interface. Syntax: show interface mgmt [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface mgmt command?
Syntax: show interface mgmt [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication command do?
The ntp authentication command enables support for authentication when communicating with an NTP server. The no form of this command disables authentication support. Syntax: ntp authentication.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication command?
Syntax: ntp authentication
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication-key command do?

The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes an authentication key. Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication-key command?
Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp disable command do?
The ntp disable command disables the NTP client on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. Syntax: ntp disable.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp disable command?
Syntax: ntp disable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp enable command do?

The ntp enable command enables the NTP client on the switch to automatically adjust the local time and date on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the NTP client. Syntax: ntp enable.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp enable command?
Syntax: ntp enable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp server command do?
The ntp server command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp server command?
Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp trusted-key command do?

The ntp trusted-key command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key. Syntax: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp trusted-key command?
Syntax: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp vrf command do?
The ntp vrf command specifies the VRF on which the NTP client communicates with an NTP server. The switch cannot function as both NTP master and client on the same VRF. Syntax: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp vrf command?
Syntax: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp associations command do?

The show ntp associations command shows the status of the connection to each NTP server. The following information is displayed for each server: • Tally code: The first character is the Tally code: ◦ ◦ ◦ ◦ (blank): No state information available (e.g. non-responding server) x: Out of tolerance (discarded by intersection algorithm).: Discarded by table overflow (not used) -: Out of tolerance (discarded by the cluster algorithm) ◦ +: Good and a preferred remote peer or server (included by the combine algorithm) ◦ #: Good remote peer or server, but not utilized (ready as a backup source) ◦ *: Remote peer or server presently used as a primary reference ◦ o: PPS peer (when the prefer peer is valid) • ID: Server number. • NAME: NTP server FQDN/IP address (Only the first 24 characters of the name are displayed). Syntax: show ntp associations [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp associations command?
Syntax: show ntp associations [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp authentication-keys command do?
The show ntp authentication-keys command shows the currently defined authentication keys. Syntax: show ntp authentication-keys [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command?
Syntax: show ntp authentication-keys [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp servers command do?
The show ntp servers command shows all configured NTP servers. Syntax: show ntp servers [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp servers command?
Syntax: show ntp servers [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp statistics command?
Syntax: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp status command do?
The show ntp status command shows the status of NTP on the switch. Syntax: show ntp status [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp status command?
Syntax: show ntp status [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a layer 2 interface?

To configure a layer 2 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. Set the interface MTU (maximum transmission unit) with the command mtu 3. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a layer 3 interface?

To configure a layer 3 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. Enable routing support with the command routing 3. Assign an IPv4 address with the command ip address, or an IPv6 address with the command IPv6 4. If required, enable support for layer 3 counters with the command l3-counters 5. If required, set the IP MTU with the command ip mtu 6. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command do?

The default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command sets an interface (or a range of interfaces) to factory default values. Associates descriptive information with an interface to help administrators and operators identify the purpose or role of an interface. The no form of this command removes a description from an interface. Enables auto-negotiation of Energy-Efficient Ethernet (EEE) on an interface. EEE Negotiation is established only on auto-link negotiation with supported link partners. Syntax: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command?
Syntax: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface command do?

The interface command switches to the config-if context for a physical port. This is where you define the configuration settings for the logical interface associated with the physical port. Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface command?
Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback command do?

The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: interface loopback <ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback command?
Syntax: interface loopback <ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IPv4 address for the current layer 3 interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>{eui64 | [tag <ID>]}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the l3-counters [rx | tx] command do?

The l3-counters [rx | tx] command enables counters on a layer 3 interface. By default, all interfaces are layer 3. To change a layer 2 interface to layer 3, use the routing command. The no form of this command, with no specification, disables both transmit and receive counters on a layer 3 interface. To disable transmit (tx) or receive (rx) counters only, specify the counter type you want to disable. Sets the MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the maximum size of a layer 2 (Ethernet) frame. Frames larger than the MTU (1500 bytes by default) are dropped and cause an ICMP fragmentation-needed message to be sent back to the originator. To support jumbo frames (frames larger than 1522 bytes), increase the MTU as required by your network. A frame size of up to 9198 bytes is supported. Syntax: l3-counters [rx | tx].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the l3-counters [rx | tx] command?
Syntax: l3-counters [rx | tx]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command do?
The show allow-unsupported-transceiver command displays configuration and status of unsupported transceivers. Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command?
Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface command do?
The show interface command displays active configurations and operational status information for interfaces. Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface dom command do?

The show interface dom command shows diagnostics information and alarm/warning flags for the optical transceivers (SFP, SFP+, QSFP+). This information is known as DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring). DOM information also consists of vendor determined thresholds which trigger high/low alarms and warning flags. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface dom command?
Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface transceiver command?
Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip interface command do?
The show ip interface command shows status and configuration information for an IPv4 interface. Syntax: show ip interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip interface command?
Syntax: show ip interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip source-interface command?
Syntax: show ip source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

syntax: show ip source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 interface command do?
The show IPv6 interface command shows status and configuration information for an IPv6 interface. Syntax: show IPv6 interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

syntax: show IPv6 source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the shutdown command do?
The shutdown command disables an interface. Interfaces are disabled by default when created. Syntax: shutdown.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
Syntax: shutdown
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLANs?

VLANs: are primarily used to provide network segmentation at layer 2. VLANs enable the grouping of users by logical function instead of physical location. They make managing bandwidth usage within networks possible by: • Allowing grouping of high-bandwidth users on low-traffic segments • Organizing users from different LAN segments according to their need for common resources and individual protocols • Improving traffic control at the edge of networks by separating traffic of different protocol types. • Enhancing network security by creating subnets to control in-band access to specific network resources VLANs are generally assigned on an organizational basis rather than on a physical basis. For example, a network administrator could assign all workstations and servers used by a particular workgroup to the same VLAN, regardless of their physical locations. Hosts in the same VLAN can directly communicate with one another. A router or a Layer 3 switch is required for hosts in different VLANs to communicate with one another.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLAN interfaces?

VLAN interfaces: Access interface An access interface carries traffic for a single VLAN ID. Access interfaces are generally used to connect end devices that do not support VLANs to the network. The devices connected to an access interface are not aware of the VLAN. Access interface can carry traffic on only one VLAN, either tagged or untagged. Example On the 6400 Switch Series, interface identification differs. This example shows ingress and egress traffic behavior for an access interface.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you create and enabling a VLAN?
To create and enabling a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the configuration context with the command config 2. Create a new VLAN with the command vlan.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you disable a VLAN?

To disable a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to configuration context for the VLAN you want to disable with the command vlan 3. Disable the VLAN with the command shutdown.

section: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Assigning a VLAN to an interface?

Assigning a VLAN to an interface: To use a VLAN, it must be assigned to an interface on the switch. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are routed (layer 3) by default when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface.

topic: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the no shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan translate <VLAN-1> <VLAN-2> command do?

The no vlan translate <VLAN-1> <VLAN-2> command defines a bidirectional VLAN translation rule that maps an external VLAN ID to an internal VLAN ID on a LAG or layer 2 interface. Applies to both incoming and outgoing traffic. The no form of this command removes an existing VLAN translation rule on the current interface. NOTE: VLAN translation and MVRP cannot be enabled on the same interface.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command assigns a VLAN ID to an trunk interface. Multiple VLAN IDs can be assigned to a trunk interface. These VLAN IDs define which VLAN traffic is allowed across the trunk interface. VLANs can be assigned only to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface. The no form of this command removes one or more VLAN IDs from a trunk interface. When the last VLAN is removed from a trunk interface, the interface continues to operate in trunk mode, and will trunk all the VLANs currently defined on the switch, and any new VLANs defined in the future. To disable the trunk interface, use the command shutdown.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command assigns a native VLAN ID to a trunk interface. By default, VLAN ID 1 is assigned as the native VLAN ID for all trunk interfaces. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned as the native VLAN. NOTE: When a native VLAN is defined, the switch automatically executes the vlan trunk allowed all command to ensure that the default VLAN is allowed on the trunk. To only allow specific VLANs on the trunk, issue the vlan trunk allowed command specifying only specific VLANs. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a trunk interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command do?

The no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command enables tagging on a native VLAN. Only incoming packets that are tagged with the matching VLAN ID are accepted. Incoming packets that are untagged are dropped except for BPDUs. Egress packets are tagged. The no form of this command removes tagging on a native VLAN.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration and firmware management?

Configuration and firmware management: Checkpoints A checkpoint is a snapshot of the running configuration of a switch and its relevant metadata during the time of creation. Checkpoints can be used to apply the switch configuration stored within a checkpoint whenever needed, such as to revert to a previous, clean configuration. Checkpoints can be applied to other switches of the same platform. A switch is able to store multiple checkpoints. Checkpoint types The switch supports two types of checkpoints: • System generated checkpoints: The switch automatically generates a system checkpoint whenever a configuration change occurs. • User generated checkpoints: The administrator can manually generate a checkpoint whenever required. Maximum number of checkpoints • Maximum checkpoints: 64 (including the startup configuration) • Maximum user checkpoints: 32 • Maximum system checkpoints: 32 User generated checkpoints User checkpoints can be created at any time, as long as one configuration difference exists since the last checkpoint was created.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto command do?

The checkpoint auto command starts auto checkpoint mode. In auto checkpoint mode, the switch temporarily saves the runtime configuration as a checkpoint only for the specified time lapse interval. Configuration changes must be saved before the interval expires, otherwise the runtime configuration is restored from the temporary checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto command?
Syntax: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Dynamic segmentation?

Dynamic segmentation: (DS) is an enterprise network solution that combines Aruba OS-CX security and networking features to dynamically place clients into network segments based on client credentials. The client network segments are dynamically carved out of the enterprise networks when on-boarding secure clients. Two options are available: • User Based Tunnels (UBT). • Virtual Network Based Tunnels (VNBT). Also called switch-to-switch dynamic segmentation. In both solutions, once authenticated (using MAC-Auth or 802.1X) an enterprise client is bound to a network role and a VLAN is associated with the role. User traffic is then placed on the VLAN (know as the role VLAN) corresponding to the role to which the user belongs. Role association is defined using the individual client authentication mode or using device-profile based authentication. The administrator must pre-configure all potential role VLANs and VRFs in all access switches (and additional configuration such as IGMP snooping on VLAN, PIM RP, etc.).
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Aruba Clear Pass Policy Manager?

Aruba Clear Pass Policy Manager: Clear Pass assigns enforcement policies and profiles containing user role information based on profiled devices or authenticated user information. How it works When first configuring the switch, the tunneling profile must be configured first. This is done using the command ubt zone. Within this context, the primary controller IP address can be configured, which should be the physical IP of one of the cluster members. Once the controller information is known on the switch and the UBT service is enabled, the switch then performs a handshake with the controller to determine its reachability and to discover the version information. When reachability is confirmed, the switch executes a switch bootstrap, and sends a bootstrap message to the controller, similar to an AP Hello between an AP and a controller. This bootstrap control packet contains user role information. Once the controller receives the message, it replies with an acknowledge message.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLAN assignment by switch?

VLAN assignment by switch: Supports only untagged UBT users Supports both tagged/untagged UBT users Controller replicates the broadcast/multicast traffic (converting bcast/mcast to unicast) and sends it to every UBT client Single dedicated multicast GRE tunnel will be established between the controller and switch for Broadcast/Multicast traffic Controller forwards all broadcast/multicast traffic to.

Specifies the IP address of the primary controller IP address for the zone. The no form of this command deletes the IP address of the primary controller. For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ubt zone <ZONE-NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> command do?

The ubt zone <ZONE-NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> command creates a user-based tunneling (UBT) zone with a specified zone name and VRF name. A UBT name is used to configure all tunneled node server properties advertised by the tunneled node feature. The no form of this command removes the specified UBT zone. Syntax: ubt zone <ZONE-NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ubt zone <ZONE-NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> command?

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ubt-client-vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no ubt-client-vlan <VLAN-ID> command specifies the UBT Client VLAN or local VLAN. This VLAN is used in local-VLAN mode only. If the UBT client VLAN is configured in VLAN-extend mode it is ignored. No other feature should be enabled on the UBT client VLAN. The no form of this command removes the VLAN to use for tunneled clients.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ubt-mode vlan-extend command do?

The ubt-mode vlan-extend command selects VLAN extended mode. When VLAN-extend mode is enabled clients are assigned to their UBT role- based VLAN in the hardware datapath. The no form of the command selects local-VLAN mode. In local-VLAN mode clients are assigned to a local switch VLAN and associated with their UBT role-based VLAN when client traffic reaches the controller. The default UBT mode is local-VLAN. Syntax: ubt-mode vlan-extend.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ubt-mode vlan-extend command?
Syntax: ubt-mode vlan-extend

Syntax: show snmp agent-port
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp community command do?
The show snmp community command displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Syntax: show snmp community.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp community command?
Syntax: show snmp community
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp system command do?
The show snmp system command displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Syntax: show snmp system.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp system command?
Syntax: show snmp system
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp trap command do?
The show snmp trap command displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Syntax: show snmp trap.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?
Syntax: show snmp trap
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command displays the VRF on which the SNMP agent service is running. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?
Syntax: show snmp vrf
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 context command do?
The show snmpv3 context command displays all configured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 context
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 users
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is a password that controls read access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server community command?
Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server host command do?

The snmp-server host command configures a trap/informs receiver to which the SNMP agent can send SNMP v1/v2c/v3 traps or v2c informs. A maximum of 30 SNMP traps/informs receivers can be configured. The no form of this command removes the specified trap/inform receiver. Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server host command?
Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?
The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contact information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP contact information. Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?
The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP location information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP location information. Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. The SNMP agent can listen on multiple VRFs. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command do?

The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: • no snmpv3 security-level auth: Sets the security level to auth-privacy. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command?
Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show copp-policy statistics command do?

The show copp-policy statistics command displays statistics for all classes, or a single class, in the active CoPP policy. Syntax: show copp-policy statistics [class <CLASS> | default-class] [vsx-peer].
