For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Aruba Central Integration?

Aruba Central Integration: The Aruba Central network management solution, a software-as-a-service subscription in the cloud, provides streamlined management of multiple network devices. Arub a OS-CX switches are able to talk to Aruba Central and utilize cloud-based management functionality. Cloud-based management functionality allows for the deployment of network devices at sites with no or few dedicated IT personnel (branch offices, retail stores, and so forth). Arub a OS-CX switches utilize secure communication protocols to connect to the Aruba Central cloud portal, and can coexist with corporate security standards, such as those mandating the use of firewalls. This feature provides: • Zero-touch provisioning.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aruba-central command do?

The aruba-central command creates or enters the Aruba Central configuration context (config-aruba-central). Disables connection to Aruba Central server. When the connection is disabled, the switch does not attempt to connect to the Aruba Central server or fetch central location from any of the three sources (CLI/Aruba Activate/DHCP). It also disconnects any active connection to the Aruba Central server. Syntax: aruba-central.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aruba-central command?
Syntax: aruba-central
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show aruba-central command do?
The show aruba-central command shows information about Aruba Central connection and the status of the Activate server connection. Syntax: show aruba-central.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show aruba-central command?
Syntax: show aruba-central
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command do?

The portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command configures the specified ports so they do not egress any packets that were received on the source port specified in interface context. The no form of this command removes the port filter setting from one or more ingress ports/LAGs. Syntax: portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command?
Syntax: portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show portfilter command do?
The show portfilter command displays filter settings for all interfaces or a specific interface. Syntax: show portfilter [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show portfilter command?
Syntax: show portfilter [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is DNS?

DNS: The Domain Name System (DNS) is the Internet protocol for mapping a hostname to its IP address. DNS allows users to enter more readily memorable and intuitive hostnames, rather than IP addresses, to identify devices connected to a network. It also allows a host to keep the same hostname even if it changes its IP address. Hostname resolution can be either static or dynamic. • In static resolution, a local table is defined on the switch that associates hostnames with their IP addresses. Static tables can be used to speed up the resolution of frequently queried hosts. • Dynamic resolution requires that the switch query a DNS server located elsewhere on the network. Dynamic name resolution takes more time than static name resolution, but requires far less configuration and management. DNS client The DNS client resolves hostnames to IP addresses for protocols that are running on the switch. When the DNS client receives a request to resolve a hostname, it can do so in one of two ways: • Forward the request to a DNS name server for resolution.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the DNS client?

To configure the DNS client, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure one or more DNS name servers with the command ip dns server 2. To resolve DNS requests by appending a domain name to the requests, either configure a single domain 3. To use static name resolution for certain hosts, associate an IP address to a host with the command ip 4. Review your DNS configuration settings with the command show ip dns. Commands shown in the procedure include: VRF Name: vrf_mgmt; VRF Name: vrf_default; VRF Name: mainvrf.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list command do?

The ip dns domain-list command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list. Syntax: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-list command?
Syntax: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns host command do?

The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns host command?
Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip dns command do?
The show ip dns command shows all DNS client configuration settings or the settings for a specific VRF. Syntax: show ip dns [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip dns command?
Syntax: show ip dns [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]

section: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for LLDP?

To configure a device profile for LLDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create an LLDP group with the command port-access lldp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to an LLDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to an LLDP group with the command 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add the LLDP group with the command associate lldp-group.

task: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

section: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for CDP?

To configure a device profile for CDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a CDP group with the command port-access cdp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to a CDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to a CDP group with the command ignore 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add a CDP group to a device profile with the command associate cdp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable a device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command allows all packets related to the LLDP BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) on a secure port. The no form of this command blocks the LLDP BPDU on a secure port. On a nonsecure port, the command has no effect. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate lldp-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The associate lldp-group <GROUP-NAME> command associates an LLDP group with a device profile. A maximum of two LLDP groups can be associated with a device profile The no form of this command removes an LLDP group from a device profile. Associates a MAC group with a device profile. A maximum of two MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a MAC group from a device profile. Associates a role with a device profile. Only one role can be associated with a device profile. For information on how to configure a role, see the port access role information in the Security Guide. The no form of this command removes a role from a device profile. Syntax: associate lldp-group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate lldp-group <GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate lldp-group <GROUP-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables a device profile. The no form of this command enables a device profile. Enables a device profile. The no form of this command disables a device profile. Syntax: disable.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the disable command?
Syntax: disable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command creates a MAC group or modifies an existing MAC group. A MAC group is used to classify connected devices based on the MAC address details, such as mask or OUI. A maximum of 32 MAC groups can be configured on the switch. A maximum of 2 MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. Each group accepts 64 match or ignore commands. 222 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide The no form of this command removes a MAC group. Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command do?

The port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command creates a new device profile and switches to the config-device-profile context. A maximum of 32 device profiles can be created. The no form of this command removes a device profile. Configures the switch to block the port until a profile match occurs for a device. This configuration is required when no security feature is enabled on the port. You must enable this mode or security on the port for local MAC match feature to operate. You must not enable both features on the same port at the same time. NOTE: You must not combine any other AAA configurations with the block-until-profile-applied mode. The no form of this command removes a rule for adding devices to a MAC group. Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the LLDP agent?

To configure the LLDP agent, follow the documented procedure: 1. By default, the LLDP agent sets the management address in all TLVs in the following order: 2. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. To customize the list, use the command lldp 3. By default, support for the LLDP-MED TLV is enabled. To customize settings, use the commands lldp 4. If required, adjust LLDP timer, holdtime, reinitialization delay, and transmit delay from their default. Commands shown in the procedure include: IP address of the loopback interface..
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp neighbors command do?
The clear lldp neighbors command clears all LLDP neighbor details. Syntax: clear lldp neighbors.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp neighbors command?
Syntax: clear lldp neighbors
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?

The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. Enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp statistics command?
Syntax: clear lldp statistics
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The lldp holdtime <TIME> command sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. The no form of this command sets the holdtime to its default value of 4. Defines the IPv4 management address of the switch which is sent in the management address TLV. One IPv4 and one IPv6 management address can be configured. Syntax: lldp holdtime <TIME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp holdtime <TIME> command?
Syntax: lldp holdtime <TIME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy] command do?

The lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy] command configures support for the LLDP-MED TLV. LLDP-MED (media endpoint devices) is an extension to LLDP developed by TIA to support interoperability between VoIP endpoint devices and other networking end- devices. The switch only sends the LLDP MED TLV after receiving a MED TLV from and connected endpoint device. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables support for the LLDP MED TLV. Configures support for the LLDP-MED TLV. Supports only civic address and emergency location information number (ELIN). Coordinate-based location is not supported. The no form of this command disables support for the LLDP MED TLV. Syntax: lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy] command?
Syntax: lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp reinit <TIME> command do?

The lldp reinit <TIME> command enables reception of LLDP information on an interface. By default, LLDP reception is enabled on all active interfaces, including the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables reception of LLDP information on an interface. Sets the amount of time (in seconds) to wait before performing LLDP initialization on an interface. The no form of this command sets the reinitialization time to its default value of 2 seconds. Selects a TLV that the LLDP agent will send and receive. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. The no form of this command stops the LLDP agent from sending and receiving a specific TLV. Syntax: lldp reinit <TIME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp reinit <TIME> command?
Syntax: lldp reinit <TIME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp timer <TIME> command do?

The lldp timer <TIME> command sets the interval (in seconds) at which local LLDP information is updated and TLVs are sent to neighboring network devices by the LLDP agent. The minimum setting for this timer must be four times the value of lldp txdelay. For example, this is a valid configuration: • lldp timer = 16 • lldp txdelay = 4 And, this is an invalid configuration: • lldp timer = 5 • lldp txdelay = 2 NOTE: When copying a saved configuration to the running configuration, the value for lldp timer is applied before the value of lldp txdelay. This can result in a configuration error if the saved configuration has a value of lldp timer that is not four times the value of lldp txdelay in the running configuration. Syntax: lldp timer <TIME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp timer <TIME> command?
Syntax: lldp timer <TIME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp tlv command?
Syntax: show lldp tlv [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp statistics command do?
The show lldp statistics command shows global LLDP statistics or statistics for a specific interface. Syntax: show lldp statistics [<INTERFACE-ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp statistics command?
Syntax: show lldp statistics [<INTERFACE-ID>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp statistics mgmt command do?
The show lldp statistics mgmt command shows LLDP statistics for the OOBM interface. Syntax: show lldp statistics mgmt.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp statistics mgmt command?
Syntax: show lldp statistics mgmt
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info command do?

The show lldp neighbor-info command displays information about neighboring devices for all interfaces or for a specific interface. The information displayed varies depending on the type of neighbor connected and the type of TLVs sent by the neighbor. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info detail command do?
The show lldp neighbor-info detail command shows detailed LLDP neighbor information for all LLDP neighbor connected interfaces. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info detail command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info mgmt command do?
The show lldp neighbor-info mgmt command displays information about neighboring devices connected to the OOBM interface. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info mgmt.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info mgmt command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info mgmt
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp local-device command do?

The show lldp local-device command shows global LLDP information advertised by the switch, as well as port-based data. If VLANs are configured on any active interfaces, the VLAN ID is only shown for trunk native or untagged VLAN IDs on access interfaces. Syntax: show lldp local-device [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp local-device command?
Syntax: show lldp local-device [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the cdp command do?
The cdp command configures CDP support globally on all active interfaces or on a specific interface. By default, CDP is enabled on all active interfaces. Syntax: cdp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the cdp command?
Syntax: cdp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp counters command do?
The clear cdp counters command clears CDP counters. Syntax: clear cdp counters.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp counters command?
Syntax: clear cdp counters
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp neighbor-info command do?
The clear cdp neighbor-info command clears CDP neighbor information. Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp command do?
The show cdp command shows CDP information for all interfaces. Syntax: show cdp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp command?
Syntax: show cdp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp neighbor-info command do?
The show cdp neighbor-info command shows CDP information for all neighbors or for CDP information on a specific interface. Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Zero Touch Provisioning?

Zero Touch Provisioning: (ZTP) enables the auto-configuration of factory default switches without a network administrator onsite. When a switch is booted from its factory default configuration, ZTP autoprovisions the switch by automatically downloading and installing a firmware file, a configuration file, or both. With ZTP, even a nontechnical user (for example: a store manager in a retail chain or a teacher in a school) can deploy devices at a site. ZTP support The switch supports standards-based Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations as follows: • The switch must be running the factory default configuration. • The switch can connect to the DHCP server from the OOBM management port. The switch can connect to the DHCP server from either the OOBM management port, or a data port on the default VLAN. • ZTP operations are supported over IPv4 connections only. IPv6 connections are not supported for ZTP operations. • You must configure the DHCP server to provide a standards-based ZTP server solution.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ztp information command do?
The show ztp information command shows information about Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations performed on the switch. Syntax: show ztp information.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ztp information command?
Syntax: show ztp information
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ztp force-provision command do?
The ztp force-provision command starts on-demand ZTP. Syntax: ztp force-provision.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ztp force-provision command?
Syntax: ztp force-provision
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mtrace <IPv4-SRC-ADDR> <IPv4-GROUP-ADDR> [lhr <IPv4-LHR-ADDR>] [ttl <HOPS>] command do?

The mtrace <IPv4-SRC-ADDR> <IPv4-GROUP-ADDR> [lhr <IPv4-LHR-ADDR>] [ttl <HOPS>] command traces the specified IPv4 source and group addresses. Syntax: mtrace <IPv4-SRC-ADDR> <IPv4-GROUP-ADDR> [lhr <IPv4-LHR-ADDR>] [ttl <HOPS>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mtrace <IPv4-SRC-ADDR> <IPv4-GROUP-ADDR> [lhr <IPv4-LHR-ADDR>] [ttl <HOPS>] command?
Syntax: mtrace <IPv4-SRC-ADDR> <IPv4-GROUP-ADDR> [lhr <IPv4-LHR-ADDR>] [ttl <HOPS>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Module Total Power Usage?

Module Total Power Usage: Shows the total power consumption of all the modules listed. Power consumption is shown in Watts. Chassis Total Power Usage Shows the total instantaneous power consumed by the entire chassis, including modules and components that do not support individual power reporting. Power consumption is shown in Watts. Chassis Total Power Available Shows the total amount of power, in Watts, that can be supplied to the chassis.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show system error-counter-monitor command do?
The show system error-counter-monitor command shows error counter statistics. Syntax: show system error-counter-monitor {basic <PORT-NUM> | extended} [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show system error-counter-monitor command?
Syntax: show system error-counter-monitor {basic <PORT-NUM> | extended} [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show usb file-system command do?

The show usb file-system command shows directory listings for a mounted USB device. When entered without the <PATH> parameter the top level directory tree is shown. Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show usb file-system command?
Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you accessing the AOS-CX Web UI?

To accessing the AOS-CX Web UI, follow the documented procedure: 1. Start a supported web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar. Use 2. Optionally a pre- and post-login message may be displayed. The message can be customized or disabled 3. At the login page, enter your user name and password credentials, then click Login 4. After you log in, the main Web UI page is displayed.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route vrf command do?
The IPv6 route vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route vrf command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vrf attach command do?

The vrf attach command attaches the interface to the VRF with the name <VRF-NAME>. The command can be entered in several different command contexts. CAUTION: The no form of the command detaches the interface from the named VRF and will remove all configurations from the interface and attach the interface to the default VRF. A warning message is displayed that prompts you whether to proceed: All Layer 3 configurations associated with the VRF will be deleted. Continue (y/n)? Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vrf attach command?
Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address command do?
The IPv6 address command sets the IPv6 address for a loopback interface. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR/MASK>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command do?

The vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command attaches a non-default VRF to a loopback. The no form of this command deletes a non-default VRF from a loopback and reattaches the default VRF. Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command?
Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface loopback command do?
The show interface loopback command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface loopback command?
Syntax: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route command do?

The ip route command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route bfd command do?

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route distance command do?

The ip route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route distance command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route tag command do?
The ip route tag command configures tag for IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes tag for IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <A.B.C.D/M> [A.B.C.D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route tag command?
Syntax: ip route <A.B.C.D/M> [A.B.C.D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route vrf command do?
The ip route vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route vrf command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route command do?
The IPv6 route command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route distance command do?

The IPv6 route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv6 static route The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route distance command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route tag command do?
The IPv6 route tag command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 route <X:X::X:X/M> [X:X::X:X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route tag command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <X:X::X:X/M> [X:X::X:X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>

section: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF on the routing switch?

To configure OSPF on the routing switch, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the OSPF instance and enter the OSPF router configuration context using the following command 2. Configure a global router ID using the following command. For command details, see router-id. Commands shown in the procedure include: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]; router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>.

task: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

section: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure external route redistribution and control?

To configure external route redistribution and control, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable route redistribution using the command redistribute 2. Optionally, modify the default metric for redistribution using the following command. For command 3. Optionally, set the cost of default-summary LSAs using the following command. For command details, 4. Optionally, set the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router.

task: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF interface authentication?

To configure OSPF interface authentication, follow the documented procedure: 1. For simple-text authentication, set the password using the following command. For command details, see 2. For MD5 authentication, set the password using the following command. For command details, see ip. Commands shown in the procedure include: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}; ip ospf authentication-key.; ip ospf authentication-key <PASSWORD>; ip ospf message-digest-key {Key ID} md5 {ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command do?

The area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command sets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas, to the default value of 1. Syntax: area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command?
Syntax: area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command creates the NSSA area (Not So Stubby Area) with <AREA-ID> if not present. If area is present and not NSSA area, this command changes the area type to NSSA area. If no-summary is used, area type will be NSSA No- Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area type as NSSA. That is, the configured area will be changed to default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> nssa no-summary command enables sending inter-area routes into NSSA, but will not unset the area as NSSA. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command summarizes the routes with the matching address or masks. This command only works for border routers. The no form of this command removes route summarization for the configured IPv4 prefix address on the ABR. When using the no form of the command with the no-advertise option, enables advertising this range to other areas. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command creates the stub area with <AREA-ID> if not present. If the area is already present and it is not a normal stub area, then this command changes the stub area type to normal. If the no-summary parameter is used, area type will be stub No-Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area as a stub type. That is, the configured area will be changed to a default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> stub no_summary command enables sending inter-area routes into the stub area, but will not unset the area as stub. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command creates an OSPF virtual link with a remote ABR and enters the vlink context. The no form of this command deletes an OSPF virtual link with the specified router ID of the remote ABR. If no <ROUTER-ID> is specified, the no form of the command sets the virtual link to the default settings. Sets the OSPF virtual-link authentication type that will be used for authentication with the remote ABR. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command do?

The authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command sets the OSPF virtual-link authentication password that is used for simple-text authentication. If the password is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the virtual-link authentication password that is used for simple-text authentication. Syntax: authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command?
Syntax: authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear ip ospf statistics command do?
The clear ip ospf statistics command clear the OSPF event statistics. Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command?
Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dead-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The dead-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet comes in for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the dead interval to default for virtual links. The default value is 40 seconds (generally four times the hello packet interval). NOTE: For proper operation, set the dead interval must be longer than the hello interval. Configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Syntax: dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dead-interval <INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate always command do?

The default-information originate always command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Syntax: default-information originate always.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate always command?
Syntax: default-information originate always
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the distance <DISTANCE> command do?

The distance <DISTANCE> command defines an administrative distance for OSPF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when multiple routes are present from different routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Enables the OSPF process, if disabled. By default the OSPF process is enabled. Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command?
Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command configures graceful restart parameters for OSPF. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default interval of 120 seconds or disables the helper mode, depending on the parameter specified. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command?
Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf area command do?

The ip ospf area command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf area command?
Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication command do?

The ip ospf authentication command sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. The no form of this command deletes the authentication type used for a particular authentication with the neighbor router and sets to null authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication-key command do?

The ip ospf authentication-key command sets the authentication password used for simple-text authentication. If the password is given in ciphertext it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the authentication password used for simple-text authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf cost command do?

The ip ospf cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default cost 1. Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf cost command?
Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?
The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf hello-interval command do?

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf keychain command do?

The ip ospf keychain command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf keychain command?
Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf network command do?

The ip ospf network command configures the OSPF network type for the interface. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default which is broadcast network. Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf network command?
Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf passive command do?

The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: ip ospf passive.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf passive command?
Syntax: ip ospf passive
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?
Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf retransmit-interval command do?

The ip ospf retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf transit-delay command do?

The ip ospf transit-delay command sets the time delay in link state transmission for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the delay in link state transmission to the default of 1 second for the OSPF interface. Sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command?
Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup for the time mentioned in seconds or for a default value of 600 seconds. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command?
Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command do?

The maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPF can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPF can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command?
Syntax: maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the passive-interface default command do?

The passive-interface default command sets the message digest password that is used for message digest authentication. If the md5 key is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the message-digest password that is used for message digest authentication Configures all OSPF interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all OSPF interfaces as active. Syntax: passive-interface default.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the passive-interface default command?
Syntax: passive-interface default
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command do?

The redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command?
Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command do?

The reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPFv2. If the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set. then the cost of all the OSPFv2 interfaces is recalculated based on the reference bandwidth and link speed of the interface. For VLAN interfaces the link speed value is taken as 1 Gbps (if the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set). The no form of this command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPF to the default of 100000 Mbps. Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command?
Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for virtual links. Enables OSPF compatibility with RFC1583 (backward compatibility). If RFC1583 compatibility is enabled, then the route cost calculation follows a different method. The no form of this command disables OSPF compatibility with RFC1583 (backward compatibility). By default the RFC1583 compatibility is disabled. Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospf command do?

The router ospf command creates an OSPF process (if not created already) on a VRF, and switches to the OSPF router instance context. Up to eight OSPF processes are supported per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPF instance. Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospf command?
Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router-id command?
Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf interface command do?
The show ip ospf interface command displays general OSPF, area, state, and configuration information. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf interface command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?
The show ip ospf lsdb command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf neighbors command do?
The show ip ospf neighbors command displays information about OSPF neighbors. 102. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf neighbors command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf routes command do?
The show ip ospf routes command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf routes command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics command do?
The show ip ospf statistics command displays OSPF statistics. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics interface command do?
The show ip ospf statistics interface command displays OSPF statistics for the OSPF-enabled interfaces. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics interface command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf virtual-links command do?

The show ip ospf virtual-links command displays the current state and parameters of the OSPF virtual links. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf virtual-links command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command do?

The timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command configures the minimum delay between receiving the same LSA from a peer. The same LSA is an LSA that contains the same LSA ID number, LSA type, and advertising router ID. If an instance of the same LSA arrives sooner before the delay expires, the LSA is dropped. Generally, the LSA arrival timer should be set to a value less than or equal to the start-time value for the command timers throttle lsa start on the neighbor. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command?
Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>

section: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command do?

The timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command configures the timers for LSA generation. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>.

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>
syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

section: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command?
Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>
syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command do?

The timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers: • start-time Is the initial delay before an SPF calculation is started. Default is 200 milliseconds. • hold-time Is the progressive backoff time to wait before next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 1000 milliseconds. If a route change event occurs during this period, the value doubles until it reaches the max-wait-time. • max-wait-time Is the maximum time to wait before the next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 5000 milliseconds. This is used to limit the SPF hold timer and also defines the time to be considered for which the OSPF LSDB has to be stable, after which the SPF throttle mechanism is reset. The no form of this command sets all the configured non-default timers to default value. Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command?
Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the transit-delay <SECONDS> command do?

The transit-delay <SECONDS> command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second for virtual links. Syntax: transit-delay <SECONDS>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the transit-delay <SECONDS> command?
Syntax: transit-delay <SECONDS>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for OSPFv3?

To configure BFD for OSPFv3, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD for all interfaces enabled for OSPFv3 by using the BFD all-interfaces command in router 2. Enable BFD for a subset of interfaces that have OSPFv3 enabled by using the IPv6 ospfv3 BFD command.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> command creates a normal area with <AREA-ID> set if not present. If area is present and is not the normal area, this command changes the area type to normal area. The no form of this command deletes the area with the <AREA-ID> specified. The area can be of any type (stub, stub no-summary, and default normal area). Syntax: area <AREA-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces which have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec ESP with authentication/encryption algorithm type and key for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces with IPsec configured at interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP configuration. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP from the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command sets the cost of default-summary LSAs announced to the stub/nssa areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default-summary LSAs announced to stub/nssa areas to the default of 1. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?
The clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command clears the OSPFv3 event statistics. Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command do?

The default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPFv3. The no form of this command sets the default metric to be used for redistributed routes into OSPFv3 to the default of 25. Disables the OSPFv3 process. By default OSPFv3 process is enabled. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPFv3 configurations. Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command?
Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPSec ESP for Vlink. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for Vlink from the specified area. Syntax: encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate command do?

The default-information originate command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Syntax: default-information originate.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate command?
Syntax: default-information originate
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Configures graceful restart for OSPFv3. By default graceful restart is enabled on the OSPFv3 router. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default of 120 seconds or disables helper mode depending on the specified parameters. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command?
Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the hello-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The hello-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the hello interval to the default value of 10 seconds for virtual links. Syntax: hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the hello-interval <INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 area command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 area command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command configures IPsec ESP. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command configures NULL ESP on an interface which disables IPsec ESP. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets to the default for the OSPFv3 interface of 10 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 network command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 network command configures the network type for the interface. By default the network type is broadcast network. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default of broadcast network. 150. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 passive command configures the interface as an OSPFv3 passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in the OSPF, but does not send or receive OSPF packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 priority command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chance it will become the designated router. Setting a priority of 0 makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default 5 seconds. 152. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command disables OSPFv3 on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPFv3 on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the transit delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second. Sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer the router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup. That is, for the time specified in seconds, or the default value of 600 seconds. To disable advertisement of the maximum metric, use the no form of the command. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command?
Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospfv3 command do?

The router ospfv3 command creates the OSPFv3 process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Only one OSPFv3 process is allowed per VRF. Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3 command?
Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command shows the OSPFv3 routing table entries for Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Border Router (ASBR). Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command shows information about OSPFv3 enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 neighbors. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command shows the OSPFv3 routing table information. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command shows OSPFv3 statistics. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command shows the OSPFv3 statistics for the OSPFv3-enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command displays the current state and parameters of the OSPFv3 virtual links. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
