messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about sFlow?

sFlow: is a technology for monitoring traffic in switched or routed networks. The sFlow monitoring system is comprised of: • An sFlow Agent that runs on a network device, such as a switch. The agent uses sampling techniques to capture information about the data traffic flowing through the device and forwards this information to an sFlow collector. • An sFlow Collector that receives monitoring information from sFlow agents. The collector stores this information so that a network administrator can analyze it to understand network data flow patterns. One sFlow collector can recieve the data from many sFlow agents. NOTE: The sFlow UDP datagrams sent to a collector are not encrypted, therefore any sensitive information contained in an sFlow sample is exposed. sFlow agent The sFlow agent on the switch provides ingress sampling of all forwarded layer 2 and layer 3 traffic on LAG and Ethernet ports. High-availability is supported (packet sampling continues to work after switch-over). The sFlow agent can communicate with up to three sFlow collectors at the same time.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the sflow command do?

The sflow command enables the sFlow agent. • • In the config context, this command enables the sFlow agent globally on all interfaces. In an config-if context, this command enables the sFlow agent on a specific interface. sFlow cannot be enabled on a member of a LAG, only on the LAG. The sFlow agent is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the sFlow agent and deletes all sFlow configuration settings, either globally, or for a specific interface. Defines the IP address of the sFlow agent to use in sFlow datagrams. This address must be defined for sFlow to function. HPE recommends that the address: • • can uniquely identify the switch is reachable by the sFlow collector • does not change with time The no form of this command deletes the IP address of the sFlow agent. Syntax: sflow.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the sflow command?
Syntax: sflow
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>] command do?

The sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>] command defines a collector to which the sFlow agent sends data. Up to three collectors can be defined. At least one collector should be defined, and it must be reachable from the switch for sFlow to work. Disables the sFlow agent, but retains any existing sFlow configuration settings. The settings become active if the sFlow agent is re-enabled. Sets the sFlow header size in bytes. The no form of this command sets the header size to the default value of 128. Syntax: sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>] command?
Syntax: sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the sflow max-datagram-size <SIZE> command do?

The sflow max-datagram-size <SIZE> command sets the maximum number of bytes that are sent in one sFlow datagram. The no form of this command sets maximum number of bytes to the default value of 1400. Defines the global polling interval for sFlow in seconds. 52 AOS-CX 10.06 IP Services Guide The no form of this command sets the polling interval to the default value of 30 seconds. Defines the global sampling rate for sFlow in number of packets. The default sampling rate is 4096, which means that one in every 4096 packets is sampled. A warning message is displayed when the sampling rate is set to less than 4096 and proceeds only after user confirmation. The no form of this command sets the sampling rate to the default value of 4096. Syntax: sflow max-datagram-size <SIZE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the sflow max-datagram-size <SIZE> command?
Syntax: sflow max-datagram-size <SIZE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show sflow command do?
The show sflow command shows sFlow configuration settings and statistics for all interfaces, or for a specific interface. Syntax: show sflow [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show sflow command?
Syntax: show sflow [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dhcp command do?

The ip dhcp command enables the DHCP client on the management interface enabling the interface to automatically obtain an IP address from a DHCP server on the network. By default, the DHCP client is enabled. Syntax: ip dhcp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dhcp command?
Syntax: ip dhcp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcp-relay command do?

The dhcp-relay command enables DHCP relay support. DHCP relay is enabled by default. DHCP relay is not supported on the management interface. The no form of this command disables DHCP relay support. Syntax: dhcp-relay.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay command?
Syntax: dhcp-relay
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command do?

The dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command enables the DHCP relay hop count increment feature, which causes the DHCP relay agent to increment the hop count in all relayed DHCP packets. Hop count is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the hop count increment feature. Enables forwarding of packets from L2 VPN clients. Forwarding is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables forwarding of packets from L2 VPN clients. Configures the behavior of DHCP relay option 82. A DHCP relay agent can receive a message from another DHCP relay agent having option 82. The relay information from the previous relay agent is replaced by default. The no form of this command disables support for DHCP relay option 82. Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command?
Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip bootp-gateway command do?

The ip bootp-gateway command configures a gateway address for the DHCP relay agent to use for DHCP requests. By default DHCP relay agent picks the lowest-numbered IP address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the gateway address. Syntax: ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip bootp-gateway command?
Syntax: ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip helper-address command do?

The ip helper-address command defines the address of a remote DHCP server or DHCP relay agent. Up to eight addresses can be defined. The DHCP agent forwards DHCP client requests to all defined servers. This command requires that you define a source IP address for DHCP relay with the command ip source- interface. The configured source IP on the VRF is used to forward DHCP packets to the server. A helper address cannot be defined on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command removes an IP helper address. Syntax: ip helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip helper-address command?
Syntax: ip helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcp-relay command do?
The show dhcp-relay command shows DHCP relay configuration settings. Syntax: show dhcp-relay [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcp-relay command?
Syntax: show dhcp-relay [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcp-relay bootp-gateway command do?
The show dhcp-relay bootp-gateway command shows the bootp gateway defined for all interfaces or a specific interface. Syntax: show dhcp-relay bootp-gateway [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcp-relay bootp-gateway command?
Syntax: show dhcp-relay bootp-gateway [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip helper-address command do?
The show ip helper-address command shows the helper IP addresses defined for all interfaces or a specific interface. Syntax: show ip helper-address [interface <INTERFACE-ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip helper-address command?
Syntax: show ip helper-address [interface <INTERFACE-ID>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the DHCPv6 relay agent?

To configure the DHCPv6 relay agent, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable the DHCPv6 agent with the command dhcpv6-relay 2. Configure one or more IP helper addresses with the command IPv6 helper-address. This determines 3. If you want to enable DHCP option 79 support to forward client link-layer addresses, use the command 4. Review DHCPv6 relay agent configuration settings with the commands show dhcpv6-relay and show 5. Enable DHCP relay 6. Define an IPv6 helper address on interfaces 1/1/1 and 1/1/2 7. Verify DHCP relay configuration. Commands shown in the procedure include: IPv6 helper-address..
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcpv6-relay command do?

The dhcpv6-relay command enables DHCPv6 relay support. DHCPv6 relay is disabled by default. DHCP relay is not supported on the management interface The no form of this command disables DHCP relay support. Enables support for DHCP relay option 79. When enabled, the DHCPv6 relay agent forwards the link-layer address of the client. This option is disabled by default. Syntax: dhcpv6-relay.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-relay command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-relay
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 helper-address command do?

The IPv6 helper-address command defines the address of a remote DHCPv6 server or DHCPv6 relay agent. Up to eight addresses can be defined. The DHCPv6 agent forwards DHCPv6 client requests to all defined servers. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command removes an IP helper address. Syntax: IPv6 helper-address unicast <UNICAST-IPv6-ADDR>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 helper-address command?
Syntax: IPv6 helper-address unicast <UNICAST-IPv6-ADDR>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv6-relay command do?
The show dhcpv6-relay command shows DHCP relay configuration settings. Syntax: show dhcpv6-relay [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv6-relay command?
Syntax: show dhcpv6-relay [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 helper-address command do?
The show IPv6 helper-address command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show IPv6 helper-address [interface <INTERFACE-ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 helper-address command?
Syntax: show IPv6 helper-address [interface <INTERFACE-ID>] [vsx-peer]

section: Configuring a DHCPv4 server on a VRF

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a DHCPv4 server on a VRF?

To configure a DHCPv4 server on a VRF, follow the documented procedure: 1. Assign the DHCPv4 server to a VRF with the command dhcp-server vrf. This switches to the DHCPv4 2. If you want the DHCPv4 server to be the sole authority for IP addresses on the VRF, enable authoritative 3. Define an address pool for the VRF with the command pool. This switches to the DHCPv4 server pool.

task: Configuring a DHCPv4 server on a VRF

section: Configuring the DHCPv6 server on a VRF

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the DHCPv6 server on a VRF?

To configure the DHCPv6 server on a VRF, follow the documented procedure: 1. If you want the DHCP server to be the sole authority for IP addresses on the VRF, enable authoritative 2. Define an address pool for the VRF with the command pool. This switches to the DHCPv6 server pool 3. Enable the DHCPv6 server on the VRF with the command enable 4. Configure support for persistent external storage of DHCP settings with the command dhcv6p-server 5. View DHCPv6 server configuration settings with the command show dhcpv6-server all-vrfs.

task: Configuring the DHCPv6 server on a VRF

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcp-server vrf VRF-NAME command do?

The dhcp-server vrf VRF-NAME command configures the DHCPv4 server to support a VRF and changes to the config-dhcp-server context for that VRF. The no form of this command removes DHCPv4 server support on a VRF. Disables the DHCPv4 server on the current VRF. The DHCPv4 server is disabled by default when configured on a VRF. Defines up to four DNS servers for the current DHCPv4 server pool. The no form of this command removes the specified DNS servers from the pool. Syntax: dhcp-server vrf VRF-NAME.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcp-server vrf VRF-NAME command?
Syntax: dhcp-server vrf VRF-NAME
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command do?

The domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command defines a domain name for the current DHCPv4 server pool. The no form of this command removes the specified domain name from the pool. Enables the DHCPv4 server on the current VRF. The DHCPv4 server is disabled by default when configured on a VRF. 86 AOS-CX 10.06 IP Services Guide Sets the length of the DHCPv4 lease time for the current pool. The lease time determines how long an IP address is valid before a DHCPv4 client must request that it be renewed. The no form of this command returns the DHCPv4 lease time to its default value 1 hour. Syntax: domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command?
Syntax: domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command do?

The netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command defines up to four NetBIOS WINS servers for the current DHCPv4 server pool. WINS is used by Microsoft DHCP clients to match host names with IP addresses. The no form of this command removes the specified WINS servers from the pool. Defines the NetBIOS node type for the current DHCPv4 server pool. The no form of this command removes the NetBIOS node type for the current pool. Defines custom DHCPv4 options for the current DHCPv4 server pool. DHCPv4 options enable the DHCPv4 server to provide additional information about the network when DHCPv4 clients request an address. The no form of this command removes custom DHCPv4 options from the pool. Syntax: netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command?
Syntax: netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the pool <POOL-NAME> command do?

The pool <POOL-NAME> command creates a DHCPv4 server pool for the current VRF and switches to the config-dhcp-server-pool context for it. Multiple pools, each with a distinct range, can be assigned to a VRF. A maximum of 64 pools (IPv4 and IPv6), 64 address ranges, and 8182 clients are supported on the switch across all VRFs. The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv4 server pool. Defines the range of IP addresses supported by the current DHCPv4 server pool. A maximum of 64 ranges are supported per switch across all VRFs. The no form of this command deletes the address range for the current pool. Syntax: pool <POOL-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the pool <POOL-NAME> command?
Syntax: pool <POOL-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcp-server command do?
The show dhcp-server command shows configuration settings for the DHCPv4 server. Syntax: show dhcp-server [all-vrfs].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcp-server command?
Syntax: show dhcp-server [all-vrfs]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the option <OPTION-NUM> {ascii <ASCII-STR> | hex <HEX-STR> | ip <IPv6-ADDR-LIST>} command do?

The option <OPTION-NUM> {ascii <ASCII-STR> | hex <HEX-STR> | ip <IPv6-ADDR-LIST>} command defines custom DHCPv6 options for the current DHCPv6 server pool. The no form of this command removes custom DHCPv6 options from the pool. Creates a DHCPv6 server pool for the current VRF and switches to the config-dhcpv6-server-pool context for it. Multiple pools, each with a distinct range, can be assigned to a VRF. A maximum of 64 pools (IPv4 and IPv6), 64 address ranges, and 8182 clients are supported on the switch across all VRFs. The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv6 server pool. Syntax: option <OPTION-NUM> {ascii <ASCII-STR> | hex <HEX-STR> | ip <IPv6-ADDR-LIST>}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the option <OPTION-NUM> {ascii <ASCII-STR> | hex <HEX-STR> | ip <IPv6-ADDR-LIST>} command?
Syntax: option <OPTION-NUM> {ascii <ASCII-STR> | hex <HEX-STR> | ip <IPv6-ADDR-LIST>}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv6-server command do?
The show dhcpv6-server command shows configuration settings for the DHCPv6 server. Syntax: show dhcpv6-server [all-vrfs].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv6-server command?
Syntax: show dhcpv6-server [all-vrfs]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear dhcpv4-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcpv4-snooping statistics command clears all DHCPv4 snooping statistics. Syntax: clear dhcpv4-snooping statistics.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear dhcpv4-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear dhcpv4-snooping statistics
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcpv4-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command do?

The dhcpv4-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command allows binding to be overwritten for the same IP address. When enabled, and a DHCP server offers a host an IP address that is already bound to an existing host in the binding table, the existing binding is overwritten for the new host if the new host is successfully able to acquire the same IP address. This overwriting is disabled by default, causing the DHCP server offers to be dropped. The no form of the command disables DHCPv4 snooping overwrite binding. Adds an authorized (trusted) DHCP server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCPv4 snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCP servers are considered to be trusted for DHCPv4 snooping purposes. Syntax: dhcpv4-snooping allow-overwrite-binding.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcpv4-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command?
Syntax: dhcpv4-snooping allow-overwrite-binding
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcpv4-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The dhcpv4-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of DHCP bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max bindings applies. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. Configures the addition of option 82 DHCP relay information to DHCP client packets that are being forwarded on trusted ports. DHCP relay is enabled by default. In the switch default state and when this command is entered without parameters (dhcpv4-snooping option 82), this default configuration is used: dhcpv4-snooping option 82 remote-id mac untrusted-policy drop When remote-id is omitted, its default (mac) is used. When untrusted-policy is omitted, its default (drop) is used. Syntax: dhcpv4-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcpv4-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
Syntax: dhcpv4-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv4-snooping command do?
The show dhcpv4-snooping command shows the DHCPv4 snooping configuration. Syntax: show dhcpv4-snooping [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv4-snooping command?
Syntax: show dhcpv4-snooping [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv4-snooping binding command do?
The show dhcpv4-snooping binding command shows the DHCPv4 snooping binding configuration. Syntax: show dhcpv4-snooping binding [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv4-snooping binding command?
Syntax: show dhcpv4-snooping binding [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv4-snooping statistics command do?
The show dhcpv4-snooping statistics command shows the DHCPv4 snooping statistics. Syntax: show dhcpv4-snooping statistics [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv4-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: show dhcpv4-snooping statistics [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command clears all DHCPv6 snooping statistics. Syntax: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCPv6 server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCPv6 snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCPv6 servers are considered to be trusted for DHCPv6 snooping purposes. NOTE: The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. NOTE: Configure the link local IPv6 address instead of global IPv6 address of the DHCPv6 server as the authorized-server. For example: switch(config)# dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server fe80: : 2ca4: fa40: d4cd: bc2f The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv6 server from the authorized list. Configures external storage to be used for backing up IPv6 bindings (used by DHCPv6 snooping) to a file. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv6-snooping command do?
The show dhcpv6-snooping command shows the DHCPv6 snooping configuration. Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv6-snooping command?
Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv6-snooping binding command do?
The show dhcpv6-snooping binding command shows the DHCPv6 snooping binding configuration. Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping binding [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv6-snooping binding command?
Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping binding [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dhcpv6-snooping statistics command do?
The show dhcpv6-snooping statistics command shows the DHCPv6 snooping statistics. Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping statistics [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dhcpv6-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: show dhcpv6-snooping statistics [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear nd-snooping statistics command do?
The clear nd-snooping statistics command clears all ND snooping statistics. Syntax: clear nd-snooping statistics.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear nd-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear nd-snooping statistics
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
Syntax: nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command do?

The nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command this command enables Routing Advertisement (RA) guard on the selected VLAN. When enabled, ingress Routing Advertisement (RA) and Routing Redirect (RR) packets on the selected VLAN are blocked on untrusted ports. The packets are forwarded when received on trusted ports. The no form of the command disables RA guard on the VLAN. NOTE: ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. This command enables Routing Advertisement (RA) drop on the selected VLAN. When enabled, ingress RA packets on the selected VLAN are blocked on both trusted and untrusted ports. When disabled, RA packets are forwarded on the selected VLAN with ND snooping trusted port validation. RA drop is disabled by default. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-guard [log].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard [log] command?
Syntax: nd-snooping ra-guard [log]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show nd-snooping [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
The show nd-snooping [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows either all ND snooping configuration or the configuration for the specified VLAN.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show nd-snooping binding command do?
The show nd-snooping binding command shows the ND snooping binding configuration. Syntax: show nd-snooping binding [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show nd-snooping binding command?
Syntax: show nd-snooping binding [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show nd-snooping prefix-list command do?
The show nd-snooping prefix-list command shows the ND snooping prefix list information. Syntax: show nd-snooping prefix-list [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show nd-snooping prefix-list command?
Syntax: show nd-snooping prefix-list [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show nd-snooping statistics command do?
The show nd-snooping statistics command shows the global ND snooping statistics. Syntax: show nd-snooping statistics [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show nd-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: show nd-snooping statistics [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about IP tunnels?

IP tunnels: True point-to-point networks are not always possible in corporate networking environment. Many networks deploy nontraditional methods of connection (for example, DSL or broadband) at remote sites or branch offices. The branch office, telecommuter, or business traveler then becomes separated from the corporate network. Some method of tunneling becomes imperative to connect all the network sites together. Virtual Private Networking (VPN) is often deployed to create private tunnels through the public network system for passing data to remote sites. While VPN is sufficient for the average business traveler, it is not a good solution for branch site connectivity. VPN configurations must include statically maintained access lists to identify traffic through the tunnel. These access lists are often tedious to configure for larger networks and are prone to errors. VPNs do not permit multicast traffic to pass; therefore routing protocols such as Routing Information Protocol (RIP) and Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) are no longer options for dynamic routing updates.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure an IP tunnel?

To configure an IP tunnel, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create an IP tunnel with the command interface tunnel 2. Optionally, set the TTL (hop count) for the tunnel with the command ttl 3. Optionally, set the MTU for the tunnel with the command ip mtu 4. Optionally, add a description to the tunnel with the command description 5. By default, the tunnel is attached to the default VRF. Attach it to a different VRF with the command vrf 6. Review tunnel settings with the command show interface tunnel.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface tunnel command do?

The interface tunnel command creates or updates an IP tunnel. After you enter the command, the firmware switches to the configuration context for the tunnel. If the specified tunnel exists, this command switches to the context for the tunnel. By default, all tunnels are automatically assigned to the default VRF when they are created. The no form of this command deletes an existing IP tunnel. Syntax: interface tunnel <TUNNEL-NUMBER> mode {gre IPv4 | ip 6in4 | ip 6in6}.

syntax: interface tunnel <TUNNEL-NUMBER> mode {gre IPv4 | ip 6in4 | ip 6in6}

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the source ip <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The source ip <IPv4-ADDR> command this command disables an IP interface. IP interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an IP interface. Sets the source IP address for an IP tunnel. Specify the IP address of a layer 3 interface on the switch. Tunnels can have the same source IP address and different destination IP addresses. The no form of this command deletes the source IP address for an IP tunnel. Sets the source IPv6 address to be used for the encapsulation. The no form of this command deletes the source IPv6 address for an IP tunnel. Syntax: source ip <IPv4-ADDR>.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about IP source lockdown?

IP source lockdown: provides added security by preventing IP source address spoofing on a per-port basis. Every packet is inspected for this purpose in hardware. When IP source lockdown is enabled, IP traffic received on an interface (port) is forwarded only if the VLAN, IP address, MAC address, interface (port) matches the IP binding database entry. NOTE: It is best to configure IP source lockdown during a switch maintenance period as enabling it may cause client traffic to be dropped for 10 to 15 seconds. To use IPv4 source lockdown, the IPv4 binding database must be populated. The binding database is typically dynamically populated by DHCPv4 snooping that learns and saves the binding information. Alternatively, the IPv4 binding database can be statically populated with the IPv4 source-binding command described in this chapter. Often DHCPv4 snooping is used to dynamically populate most of the IP binding database along with the IPv4 source-binding command that is used to add the binding information for several known and trusted clients, typically administrators.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv4 source-lockdown command do?

The IPv4 source-lockdown command enables IPv4 source lockdown for all VLANs on the selected interface (port). The no form of this command disables IPv4 source lockdown for the selected interface (port). Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv4 source-lockdown command?
Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv4 source-binding command do?

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME> command do?

The no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE: Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping.

The show IPv6 source-binding command shows all IPv6 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Syntax: show IPv6 source-binding [vsx-peer].

The no form of this command disables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages from the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. The arp cache-limit <LIMIT> command specifies the maximum number of entries in the ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) cache. The no form of this command disables Dynamic ARP Inspection on the VLAN. 188 AOS-CX 10.06 IP Services Guide. Syntax: arp cache-limit <LIMIT>.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the Interface state command do?

The Interface state command a - Active An active LACP interface. C - Collecting Data frames are received through the aggregate link and sent onto the intended destination. D - Distributing Data frames are transmitted through the aggregate link to reach the intended destination. F - Aggregable The link can be used as part of an aggregate. E - Default neighbor state The link has the default state of the neighbor switch. I - Individual The link is used as an individual link. L - Long-timeout With the long timeout, an LACPDU is sent every 30 seconds. If no response comes from its partner after three LACPDUs are sent (90 seconds), a timeout event occurs. The LACP state machine then transitions to the appropriate state based on its current state.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration consistency requirements?

Configuration consistency requirements: • Configure at least one active mode aggregation in two devices. • • For a successful static aggregation, make sure the ports at both ends of each link are in the same aggregation state. For a successful dynamic aggregation, make sure the peer ports of the ports aggregated at one end are also aggregated, and that one of the ends is configured as "active". The two ends can automatically negotiate the aggregation state of each member port. Removing interfaces • Deleting an aggregate interface also deletes its aggregation group and causes all member ports to leave the aggregation group. • When a member interface is removed from a LAG: ◦ 6100, 6200, 6300, and 6400 switches: The interface goes to its default status of unshut. ◦ 8320, 8325, 8360, or 8400 switches: The interface becomes disabled. Disabling an interface When an interface LAG is disabled with the shutdown command, all its members also become operationally down. Layer 2 aggregation groups All switches support static and dynamic layer 2 aggregation groups.

This configuration must be done at the global level. Sets an LACP mode to active or passive. The no form of this command sets the LACP mode to off, returning the LAG to a static mode aggregation. Sets the LACP port ID value of the member interface of the LAG. The no form of this command removes the LACP port ID value from the interface. For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what monitoring behavior is documented for Monitoring hardware through visual observation?

Monitoring hardware through visual observation: Confirming normal operation of the switch by reading LEDs This task describes using the switch LEDs to confirm that the switch is operating normally. Procedure 1. Quick check: Verify that the chassis has power and there are no fault conditions. On the front of the switch, verify that the states of the following LEDs are On Green: • Power • Health 2. Verify that the Health LEDs of all installed line modules are On Green. 3. Verify that the Health LEDs of all installed management modules are On Green. 4. Verify that the network ports are operating normally. a. On the active management module, check the Status Front section. Verify that each LED that indicates a line module is in one of the following states: • On Green (normal operation) • Off (no line module installed) b. On each line module, verify that each port LED is in one of the following states: • On Green, Half-Bright Green, or Flickering Green (normal operation) • Off (no cable connected or port off by default in config) 5. Verify that the power supplies are operating normally. a.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what monitoring behavior is documented for Module Total Power Usage?

Module Total Power Usage: Shows the total power consumption of all the modules listed. Power consumption is shown in Watts. Chassis Total Power Usage Shows the total instantaneous power consumed by the entire chassis, including modules and components that do not support individual power reporting. Power consumption is shown in Watts. Chassis Total Power Available Shows the total amount of power, in Watts, that can be supplied to the chassis.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what monitoring behavior is documented for IP-SLA?

IP-SLA: The IP Service Level Agreement (IP-SLA) is a feature that enables the measuring of network performance between two nodes in a network for different service level agreement parameters such as round-trip time (RTT), one-way delay, jitter, reachability, packet loss, and voice quality scores. These two nodes can span across area in access, distribution or core inside a LAN as well as across WAN between core to core or core to Data Centre switches. This feature helps you measure the SLA for different protocols or applications such as UDP echo, UDP jitter (for voice and video), TCP connect, HTTP, and ICMP echo. This guide provides details for managing and monitoring different types of IP-SLAs. IP-SLA guidelines • Arub a OS-CX supports only SLA configuration through CLI and thresholds can be configured using NAE agents using WebUI/REST. • Arub a OS-CX supports only forever tests. On-demand tests are not supported. • Maximum sessions: IP-SLA source 500, IP-SLA responder 100. • NAE can effectively monitor a maximum of 300 parameters, reducing the maximum supported session by 300.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip-sla command do?

The ip-sla command creates an IP Service Level Agreement (SLA) profile and switches to the config-ip-sla context. The no form of this command deletes an IP-SLA profile. By default, all profile use the default VRF (default). Syntax: ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip-sla command?
Syntax: ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip-sla responder command do?

The ip-sla responder command selects the IP-SLA responder. The responder can be configured for udp-echo, tcp-connect, udp-jitter-voip type. It requires the SLA name, SLA type, and port number as arguments. Source IP/interface ID is a must for type udp-jitter-voip and optional for other types. The no form of this command removes the IP-SLA responder. Syntax: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> {udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip} <PORT-NUM>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip-sla responder command?
Syntax: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> {udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip} <PORT-NUM>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip-sla responder command do?
The show ip-sla responder command shows the given IP-SLA responder configuration and operation status. Syntax: show ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip-sla responder command?
Syntax: show ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip-sla responder results command do?

The show ip-sla responder results command shows the given ip-sla responder statistics for a given source IP and port. This command is only applicable for the sources where source IP and port are configured. Syntax: show ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR> <PORT-NUM> results.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip-sla responder results command?
Syntax: show ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR> <PORT-NUM> results
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip-sla <SLA-NAME> command do?
The show ip-sla <SLA-NAME> command shows the given IP-SLA source configuration and status. Syntax: show ip-sla <SLA-NAME> results.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip-sla <SLA-NAME> command?
Syntax: show ip-sla <SLA-NAME> results
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the start-test command do?
The start-test command starts the IP-SLA probes. Stops the IP-SLA probes. Syntax: start-test.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the start-test command?
Syntax: start-test
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface downshift-enable command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] downshift-enable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config interface command do?
The show running-config interface command displays active configurations of various switch interfaces. Syntax: show running-config interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config interface command?
Syntax: show running-config interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what monitoring behavior is documented for Mirroring?

Mirroring: Mirroring allows you to replicate all traffic arriving and/or leaving the selected system interfaces. This data can be used for collection or analysis. The traffic replicated using mirroring can be sent to a separate interface on the same switch as the traffic source for analysis or inspection. Such a collection of interfaces and settings is called a mirror session. A mirror session can be configured with many traffic sources but only a single output, or destination. In the initial configuration, the mirror session is disabled. You have enable the feature to start the replication. CAUTION: Care must be taken in choosing the number and rates of sources to avoid over- saturating a session destination. A mirror session with multiple 10G sources can overwhelm a single 10G destination and important data may be lost. Mirror statistics Mirror statistics are reset for a Mirror-to-CPU session when an interface is added or removed from a LAG that is a source interface in the Mirror session and during a failover.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear mirror command do?

The clear mirror command clears the mirror statistics for all configured mirror sessions or a specified session Specifies a descriptive comment for the mirroring session. The no form of this command removes the comment. Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear mirror command?
Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy tshark-pcap command do?

The copy tshark-pcap command copies the tshark capture data to a file on a TFTP or SFTP server. The command causes the mirror session to transmit mirrored packets to the switch CPU. This destination may be configured for multiple sessions, however only one such configured session may be active at a given time. The diagnostic utility Tshark may be used to view and capture packets transmitted to the CPU through this route. Ctrl+C must be entered to terminate a Tshark capture session. More details can be found in the Supportability Guide. The no form of this command will immediately stops mirroring traffic to the CPU, but will not remove any sources from the mirror configuration. Configures the specified interface as the destination of the mirrored traffic. Syntax: copy tshark-pcap <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy tshark-pcap command?
Syntax: copy tshark-pcap <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the diagnostic command do?

The diagnostic command specifies the tunnel where all mirrored traffic for the session is transmitted. Only one tunnel destination is allowed per session. You may configure multiple mirror sessions with the same source/destination IP address pair, however, only one of those sessions sharing the same source/destination IP address pair can be enabled at a given time. ERSPAN is not supported leaving the switch by the OOB port. If VRF management is configured for an ERSPAN session, the session will be in "mirror_err_tunnel_oob_port_not_supported" operation status. ERSPAN is not supported leaving the switch encapsulated within another tunnel (e.g. GRE IPv4). When the path to the destination IP address will leave via a tunnel, the session will be in "tunnel_route_resolution_not_populated" operation status. Syntax: diagnostic.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the diagnostic command?
Syntax: diagnostic
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show mirror command do?

The show mirror command shows information about mirroring sessions. If <SESSION-ID> is not specified, then the command shows a summary of all configured mirroring sessions. If <SESSION-ID> is specified, then the command shows detailed information about the specified mirroring session. Syntax: show mirror [<SESSION-ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show mirror command?
Syntax: show mirror [<SESSION-ID>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the source interface {<PORT-NUM> | <LAG-NAME>} [<DIRECTION>] command?
Syntax: source interface {<PORT-NUM> | <LAG-NAME>} [<DIRECTION>]

section: Monitoring a device by using SNMP

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what monitoring behavior is documented for Monitoring a device by using SNMP?

Monitoring a device by using SNMP: Configuring SNMP: Refer to the Arub a OS-CX SNMP/MIB Guide for information on how to add SNMP so a device can be monitored from a network management system (NMS). Configuring an SNMP trap receiver: Refer to the Arub a OS-CX SNMP/MIB Guide and specific information about the show snmp trap command to enable SNMP traps.

topic: Monitoring a device by using SNMP

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp dot3 poe command do?

The lldp dot3 poe command enables 802.3 TLV list in LLDP to advertise for Power over Ethernet Data Link Layer Classification. LLDP dot3 TLV is by default enabled for PoE. The no form of this command disables 802.3 TLV list in LLDP. Syntax: lldp dot3 poe.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp dot3 poe command?
Syntax: lldp dot3 poe
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp med poe [priority-override] command do?

The lldp med poe [priority-override] command enables MED TLV list in LLDP to advertise for Power over Ethernet Data Link Layer Classification. Also enables the lldp-MED TLV priority to override user configured port priority for Power over Ethernet. When both dot3 and MED are enabled, dot 3 will take precedence. MED TLV is by default enabled for PoE. Priority over-ride is by default disabled. The no form of this command disables MED TLV list in LLDP. Enables per-interface power distribution. Per-port power is enabled by default with priority low. PoE cannot be disabled for individual ports when Quick PoE is enabled for the entire switch or line module. The no form of this command disables per-interface power distribution. 124 AOS-CX 10.06 Monitoring Guide Configures the power allocation method. Syntax: lldp med poe [priority-override].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp med poe [priority-override] command?
Syntax: lldp med poe [priority-override]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command do?

The power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command sets PoE priority for an interface Specifying critical, high, or low indicates the priority of the interface in the event of power over-subscription. Within the same priority level, higher power-priority line-module ports have higher precedence. With same PoE priority and same line-module priority, lower numbered line- module ports have higher precedence. Per-interface PoE priority is low by default. The no form of this command resets the priority to default PoE priority "low". Quick PoE is a feature that provides the ability for the switch to provide power to the connected powered device as soon as switch goes through cold reboot. When quick PoE is enabled on the subsystem PoE port 128 AOS-CX 10.06 Monitoring Guide disablement and PD demotion is not allowed. Syntax: power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor command do?
The show lldp neighbor command displays detailed information about a particular neighbor connected to a particular interface. Syntax: show lldp neighbor [<INTERFACE-ID>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor [<INTERFACE-ID>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Multicast?

Multicast: Introduction to multicast Multicast addressing allows one-to-many or many-to-many communication among hosts on a network. Typical applications of multicast communication include: audio and video streaming, desktop conferencing, collaborative computing, and similar applications. In a network where IP multicast traffic is transmitted for multimedia applications, such traffic is blocked at routed interface (VLAN) boundaries unless a multicast routing protocol is running. Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) is a family of routing protocols that form multicast trees to forward traffic from multicast sources to subnets that have used a protocol such as IGMP to request the traffic. PIM relies on the unicast routing tables created by any of several unicast routing protocols to identify the path back to a multicast source (Reverse Path Forwarding, or RPF). With this information, PIM sets up the distribution tree for the multicast traffic. IGMP provides the multicast traffic link between a host and a multicast router running PIM- SM.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP)?

Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP): In a network where IP multicast traffic is transmitted for various multimedia applications, you can use the switch to reduce unnecessary bandwidth usage on a per-port basis by configuring IGMP (Internet Group Management Protocol). IGMPv3 (RFC 3376) and IGMPv2 (RFC 2236) are the current RFCs for IGMP. In the factory default state (IGMP disabled), the switch simply floods all IP multicast traffic it receives on a given VLAN through all ports on that VLAN (except the port on which it received the traffic). This can result in significant and unnecessary bandwidth usage in networks where IP multicast traffic is a factor. Enabling IGMP allows the ports to detect IGMP queries and report packets and manage IP multicast traffic through the switch. IGMP is useful in multimedia applications such as LAN TV, desktop conferencing, and collaborative computing, where there is Multi Point communication; that is, communication from one to many hosts, or communication originating from many hosts and destined for many other hosts.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp command do?
The ip igmp command enables or disables IGMP on the current interface. IGMP is disabled by default. Syntax: ip igmp {enable | disable}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp command?
Syntax: ip igmp {enable | disable}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp apply access-list command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp apply access-list command?
Syntax: ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp last-member-query-interval command do?

The ip igmp last-member-query-interval command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval command?
Syntax: ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier interval command do?

The ip igmp querier interval command configures the interval between IGMP queries on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier interval to the default value of 125 seconds on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier interval command?
Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command do?

The ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command configures the IGMP querier max response time value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the querier max response time value to the default of 10 seconds on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command?
Syntax: ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp robustness command do?

The ip igmp robustness command configures IGMP robustness on an interface, depending on the command context. The robustness parameter allows tuning for the expected packet loss on a subnet. The no form of this command sets the robustness value to the default of 2 on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp robustness <VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp robustness command?
Syntax: ip igmp robustness <VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp static-group command do?

The ip igmp static-group command configures an IGMP static multicast group on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. You can configure a maximum of 32 IGMP static groups. The no form of the command unconfigures IGMP static multicast group on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp static-group <MULTICAST-GROUP-IP>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp static-group command?
Syntax: ip igmp static-group <MULTICAST-GROUP-IP>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp version command do?
The ip igmp version command configures the IGMP version on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. Syntax: ip igmp version <VERSION>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp version command?
Syntax: ip igmp version <VERSION>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp version strict command do?

The ip igmp version strict command configures an IGMP strict version on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. Drops packets that do not match the configured version. The no form of the command removes the strict version configuration from the interface. Syntax: ip igmp version <VERSION> strict.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp version strict command?
Syntax: ip igmp version <VERSION> strict
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ip igmp command do?
The no ip igmp command disables all IGMP configurations on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. Syntax: no ip igmp.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no ip igmp command?
Syntax: no ip igmp
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp command?
Syntax: show ip igmp [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp counters command do?
The show ip igmp counters command shows IGMP counter details, or shows counters by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp counters [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp counters command?
Syntax: show ip igmp counters [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp group command do?

The show ip igmp group command shows IGMP joined group information for the specified group, or shows joined group source and display information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp group <GROUP-IP> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp group command?
Syntax: show ip igmp group <GROUP-IP> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp groups command do?
The show ip igmp groups command shows IGMP group information, or you can display group information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp groups [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp groups command?
Syntax: show ip igmp groups [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp interface command?
Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID>|vlan <VLAN-ID>} [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface counters command do?
The show ip igmp interface counters command shows IGMP counter details for a specific interface or VLAN interface. Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} counters [vsx-peer].

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} counters [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp interface counters command?
Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} counters [vsx-peer]

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} counters [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface group command do?

The show ip igmp interface group command shows IGMP joined group information for a specific interface or VLAN interface, or specify a source IP. Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} group <GROUP-ID> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [vsx-peer].

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} group <GROUP-ID> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp interface group command?
Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} group <GROUP-ID> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [vsx-peer]

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} group <GROUP-ID> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface groups command do?
The show ip igmp interface groups command shows IGMP group information for a specific interface or VLAN interface. Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} groups [vsx-peer].

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} groups [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp interface groups command?
Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} groups [vsx-peer]

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} groups [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface statistics command do?

The show ip igmp interface statistics command shows IGMP statistics for a specific interface or VLAN interface, including groups joined. Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} statistics [vsx-peer].

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} statistics [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp interface statistics command?
Syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} statistics [vsx-peer]

syntax: show ip igmp interface {<INTF-ID> | vlan <VLAN-ID>} statistics [vsx-peer]

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp static-groups command do?
The show ip igmp static-groups command shows IGMP static groups, or shows information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp static-groups [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp static-groups command?
Syntax: show ip igmp static-groups [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp statistics command do?
The show ip igmp statistics command shows IGMP statistics, including groups joined, or shows statistics by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp statistics [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp statistics command?
Syntax: show ip igmp statistics [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command do?
The ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command enables or disables IGMP snooping on the VLAN. By default, IGMP snooping is disabled. Syntax: ip igmp snooping {enable | disable}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping {enable | disable}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping apply access-list command do?

The ip igmp snooping apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. Syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping apply access-list command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping auto command do?

The ip igmp snooping auto command configures the specified ports in auto mode. In auto mode traffic flow is controlled by the IGMP joins/leaves. Auto mode is the default. The no form of this command removes auto mode ports for the VLAN. 54 AOS-CX 10.06 Multicast Guide. Syntax: ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping auto command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping blocked command do?

The ip igmp snooping blocked command configures the specified ports in blocked mode. In blocked mode, joins and traffic are always blocked on this port. The no form of this command disables blocked ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping blocked <PORT-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping blocked command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping blocked <PORT-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command do?

The ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command configures drop-unknown mode. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. The no form of this command disables drop unknown on the switch. Syntax: ip igmp snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastlearn command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastlearn command enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. The no form of this command disables fast learn on the specified ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastleave command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastleave command enables the switch to immediately remove the IGMP client from its IGMP table and cease transmitting multicast traffic to the client. The no form of this command disables fastleave on the specified ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastleave command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping forward command do?

The ip igmp snooping forward command configures the specified ports in forward mode. In forward mode, traffic is always forwarded on this port, irrespective of joins. The no form of this command disables forward ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping forward command?
Syntax: ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping static-group command do?

section: Protocol Independent Multicast - Sparse Mode (V4 and V6)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Protocol Independent Multicast - Sparse Mode (V4 and V6)?

Protocol Independent Multicast - Sparse Mode (V4 and V6): Protocol Independent Multicast - Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) overview In a network, IP multicast traffic transmitted for multimedia applications is blocked at routed interface boundaries unless a multicast routing protocol is running. Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) is a family of routing protocols. It forms multicast trees to forward traffic from multicast sources to subnets which use protocols such as IGMP and MLD to request the traffic. PIM relies on the unicast routing tables to identify the path back to a multicast source (reverse path forwarding (RPF)). The unicast routing protocols create the unicast routing tables. With this information, PIM sets up the distribution tree for the multicast traffic. PIM-Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) can be configured on physical ports, VLAN interfaces, LAG interfaces, and loopback interfaces. All such configurations work in the mentioned interfaces context. IGMP/MLD provides the multicast traffic link between a host and a multicast router running PIM-SM.

topic: Protocol Independent Multicast - Sparse Mode (V4 and V6)

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Routing protocol support?

Routing protocol support: PIM uses unicast routing information from any of the routing protocols that are running on the system, such as OSPFv2, OSPFv3, BGP. Static routes are also supported with Nexthop IP addresses. Max interface support per flow Up to 127 outbound interfaces (and 1 inbound interface) are supported in the multicast routing table at any given time. The sum of all outbound interfaces across all current flows on a router may not exceed 127.

section: Configuring PIM-SM options in an interface

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure PIM-SM options in an interface?

To configure PIM-SM options in an interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the frequency at which the router transmits PIM hello messages on the current interface using 2. Change the maximum time before the router transmits the initial PIM hello message on the interface. Commands shown in the procedure include: ip pim-sparse hello-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>; IPv6 pim6-sparse hello-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>; ip pim-sparse hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>; IPv6 pim6-sparse hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the accept-register access-list <ACL-RULE> command do?

The accept-register access-list <ACL-RULE> command configures ACL on RP to filter PIM Register packets from unauthorized sources. The ACL specified will contain the (S, G) traffic in register packets to permitted or denied. The no form of this command removes the currently configured ACL rule. Enables PIM router to filter PIM join/prune messages destined for a specific RP and specific groups. The ACL specifies the group addresses which are allowed or denied. Up to 8 RP addresses and group ACL can be associated with the PIM router. The no form of this command removes the currently configured ACL rule. Syntax: accept-register access-list <ACL-RULE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the accept-register access-list <ACL-RULE> command?
Syntax: accept-register access-list <ACL-RULE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all PIM interfaces. BFD can be disabled at individual PIM interface using the ip pim- sparse bfd disable command. The no form of this command disables BFD for all the interfaces. Configures the interval in seconds to send periodic RP-Set messages to all PIM-SM interfaces on a router that operates as the BSR in a domain. This setting must be smaller than the rp-candidate hold-time settings (range of 30 to 255; default 150) configured in the RPs operating in the domain. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets it to the default of 60 seconds. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
Syntax: bfd all-interfaces
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bsr-candidate hash-mask-length <LENGTH-VALUE> command do?

The bsr-candidate hash-mask-length <LENGTH-VALUE> command controls the distribution of multicast groups among the C-RP, in a domain where there is overlapping coverage of the groups among the RPs. This value specifies the length (number of significant bits) when allocating this distribution. A longer hash-mask-length results in fewer multicast groups, for each block of group addresses assigned to the RPs. Multiple blocks of addresses assigned to each C-RP results in wider dispersal of addresses. Includes enhanced load-sharing for the multicast traffic for the different groups that are used in the domain at the same time. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 30. Configures the priority to apply to the router when a BSR election process occurs in the PIM-SM domain. Syntax: bsr-candidate hash-mask-length <LENGTH-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bsr-candidate hash-mask-length <LENGTH-VALUE> command?
Syntax: bsr-candidate hash-mask-length <LENGTH-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse {enable disable} command do?

The ip pim-sparse {enable disable} command enables or disables PIM-SM in the current interface. PIM-SM is disabled by default on an interface. IP address must be configured on the interface to enable PIM-SM. Syntax: ip pim-sparse {enable|disable}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse {enable disable} command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse {enable|disable}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse bfd command do?

The ip pim-sparse bfd command configures BFD on a per-interface basis for one interface associated with the PIM process. The no form of this command removes the BFD configuration on the interface and sets it to the default configuration. NOTE: If BFD is enabled globally, it will be enabled by default on all interfaces. The only exception is when it is disabled specifically on an interface using the ip pim-sparse bfd disable command. If BFD is disabled globally, it will be disabled by default on all interfaces. The only exception is when it is enabled specifically on an interface using the ip pim-sparse bfd command. Syntax: ip pim-sparse bfd [disable].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse bfd command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse bfd [disable]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse dr-priority command do?

The ip pim-sparse dr-priority command changes the router priority for the designated router (DR) election process in the current interface. A numerically higher value means a higher priority. If multiple routes share the highest priority, the router with the highest IP address is selected as the DR. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 1. Syntax: ip pim-sparse dr-priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse dr-priority command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse dr-priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse hello-delay command do?

The ip pim-sparse hello-delay command configures the maximum time in seconds before the router actually transmits the initial PIM hello message on the current interface. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 5 seconds. Syntax: ip pim-sparse hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse hello-delay command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse hello-interval command do?

The ip pim-sparse hello-interval command configures the frequency at which the router transmits PIM hello messages on the current interface. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets to the default of 30 seconds. Syntax: ip pim-sparse hello-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse hello-interval command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse hello-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse ip-addr command do?

The ip pim-sparse ip-addr command enables the router to dynamically determine the source IP address to use for PIM-SM packets sent from the interface or to use the specific IP address. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets to the default of any. Syntax: ip pim-sparse ip-addr {<IP-ADDR-VALUE> | any}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse ip-addr command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse ip-addr {<IP-ADDR-VALUE> | any}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse lan-prune-delay command do?

The ip pim-sparse lan-prune-delay command enables the LAN prune delay option on the current interface. The default is enabled. With LAN-prune-delay enabled, the router informs downstream neighbors how long it will wait before pruning a flow after receiving a prune request. Other downstream routers on the same interface must send a join to override the prune before the LAN-prune-delay time to continue the flow. Prompts any downstream neighbors with multicast receivers continuing to belong to the flow to reply with a join. If no joins are received after the LAN-prune-delay period, the router prunes the flow. The propagation-delay and override-interval settings determine the LAN-prune-delay setting. The no form of this command disables the LAN prune delay option. Syntax: ip pim-sparse lan-prune-delay.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse lan-prune-delay command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse lan-prune-delay
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse override-interval command do?

The ip pim-sparse override-interval command configures the override interval that gets inserted into the Override Interval field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets the value to the default of 2500 ms. Syntax: ip pim-sparse override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse override-interval command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse override-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip pim-sparse propagation-delay command do?

The ip pim-sparse propagation-delay command configures the propagation delay that gets inserted into the LAN prune delay field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 500 ms. Configures the frequency at which the router will send periodic join or prune-interval messages. The no form of this command sets the interval to the default value of 60 seconds. Syntax: ip pim-sparse propagation-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip pim-sparse propagation-delay command?
Syntax: ip pim-sparse propagation-delay <DELAY-VALUE>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ip pim-sparse command do?

The no ip pim-sparse command removes all the PIM-SM related configurations for the interface. Configures the limit on the maximum number of register messages sent per second for every unique (S, G) entry. By default, there is no maximum rate set. When the limit is configured, register messages generation is limited to the configured value. The no form of this command removes the currently configured limit value. Syntax: no ip pim-sparse.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no ip pim-sparse command?
Syntax: no ip pim-sparse
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router pim command do?

The router pim command changes the current context to the PIM configuration context. If no VRF is specified, the default VRF is assumed. The no form of this command removes the PIM configuration from the specified context or the default VRF. Statically configures the router as the RP for a specified multicast group or range of multicast groups. This must be configured on all PIM-SM routers in the domain. If group address is not specified, it applies to all IPv4 multicast addresses (224.0.0.0 - 239.255.255.255). PIM-SM supports a maximum of 8 static RPs per VRF. The no form of this command removes static RP configuration and its precedence. Syntax: router pim [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router pim command?
Syntax: router pim [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the rp-candidate group-prefix <GRP-ADDR/GRP-MASK> command do?

The rp-candidate group-prefix <GRP-ADDR/GRP-MASK> command adds multicast group address to the current Candidate Rendezvous Point (C-RP) configuration. The no form of this command removes C-RP multicast group address. Changes the hold-time a C-RP includes in its advertisements to the BSR. Hold-time is included in the advertisements the C-RP periodically sends to the elected BSR for the domain. Also updates the BSR on how long to wait after the last advertisement from the reporting RP before assuming it has become unavailable. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets it to the default value 150 seconds. Changes the current priority setting for a C-RP. Where multiple C-RP configurations are used to support the same multicast groups, the candidate having the highest priority is elected. Syntax: rp-candidate group-prefix <GRP-ADDR/GRP-MASK>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the rp-candidate group-prefix <GRP-ADDR/GRP-MASK> command?
Syntax: rp-candidate group-prefix <GRP-ADDR/GRP-MASK>

The show ip mroute command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip mroute [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

Syntax: show ip pim bsr [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]

The show ip pim bsr elected command shows information about the elected BSR in the domain and multicast groups it supports. Optionally you can specify display information by VRF. If no options are specified, it shows information for the default VRF. Syntax: show ip pim bsr elected [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
