section: Best practices for loop protect over VSX

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Best practices for loop protect over VSX?

Best practices for loop protect over VSX: • Enable loop protect on both primary and secondary VSX switches. • Do not enable loop protect on the ISL link for the primary and secondary VSX switches. • • If you enable an action for loop protect, such as do-not-disable, and another action, such as Tx-Rx- Disable, is already in effect, loop protect must be disabled and then re-enabled. If you run the loop-protect action do-not-disable command, on every transmit interval, the loop is detected and the detection is reported through an SNMP trap and an event log message. You can view the events for just the loop protect feature by entering the show events -d hpe-lpd command. • The total number of VLANs across ports is (ports x VLANs) = 4094 ports per VLAN. Loop protect can be configured on a maximum of 4094 VLANs across all interfaces without updating CoPP policies for loop protect. If your network configuration requires you to configure more VLAN, update your CoPP policies values for loop protect to ensure that you allocate more resources.

topic: Best practices for loop protect over VSX

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about EVPN VSX support?

EVPN VSX support: Ethernet VPN (EVPN) is supported with VSX. The two VSX pairs act as independent BGP routing entities to the other VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) or spines for control packets. However, in the datapath, both of them act as a single logical VTEP. This is achieved by using different IP addresses for establishing the BGP session and using a common IP as next-hop to represent the VTEP. For more information on EVPN VSX support, see the EVPN VSX support chapter in the VXLAN Guide.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the config-sync disable command do?

The config-sync disable command pauses VSX synchronization. The no form of this command restarts VSX synchronization. Configures a physical port or a LAG as an interswitch link port. Only one port or LAG can be configured to act as an ISL. Once a port is configured as an ISL, it becomes a part of all VLANs in a system. The no form of this command clears the configuration of the interswitch link port from a physical port or a LAG. Syntax: config-sync disable.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the config-sync disable command?
Syntax: config-sync disable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the inter-switch-link dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL> command do?

The inter-switch-link dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL> command sets the dead interval for the interswitch link protocol. The dead interval is the amount of time to wait for hellos from a peer before declaring the peer to be dead. The default dead interval time is 20 seconds. The no form of this command resets the interswitch link dead interval to the default of 20 seconds. Syntax: inter-switch-link dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the inter-switch-link dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: inter-switch-link dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the inter-switch-link hello-interval <HELLO-INTERVAL> command do?

The inter-switch-link hello-interval <HELLO-INTERVAL> command configures the interswitch link hello-interval. The hello interval determines the frequency of a hello packet exchange to confirm the control plane of the peer is alive. The default hello-interval is 1 second. The no form of this command sets the interswitch link hello-interval to the default of 1 second. Sets the holdtime for the interswitch link protocol. A port is treated as down only when it stays down for the configured holdtime interval. The default holdtime is 0 seconds. 114. Syntax: inter-switch-link hello-interval <HELLO-INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the inter-switch-link hello-interval <HELLO-INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: inter-switch-link hello-interval <HELLO-INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the inter-switch-link peer-detect-interval <PEER-DETECT-INTERVAL> command do?

The inter-switch-link peer-detect-interval <PEER-DETECT-INTERVAL> command sets the amount of time in seconds that the VSX switch waits for the ISL interface to link up after a reboot. If the ISL link does not come up within this time window, the VSX switch declares itself as split from its peer. The default peer detect interval is 300 seconds. The no form of this command sets the interswitch link protocol peer detect interval to the default of 300 seconds. Syntax: inter-switch-link peer-detect-interval <PEER-DETECT-INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the inter-switch-link peer-detect-interval <PEER-DETECT-INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: inter-switch-link peer-detect-interval <PEER-DETECT-INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface lag multi-chassis command do?

The interface lag multi-chassis command configures a given LAG as a dynamic multichassis LAG (VSX LAG), which supports a maximum of four member links per switch segment. A VSX LAG across a downstream switch can have at most a total of eight member links. The no form of this command removes a VSX LAG. Syntax: interface lag <LAG-ID> multi-chassis [static].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface lag multi-chassis command?
Syntax: interface lag <LAG-ID> multi-chassis [static]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the keepalive dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL> command do?

The keepalive dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL> command sets the dead-interval for keepalive protocol. The dead interval is the amount of time to wait for hellos from a peer before declaring the peer to be dead. The default dead-interval is 3 seconds. The no form of this command sets the interswitch link dead-interval to the default of 3 seconds. Sets the hello-interval for keepalive protocol. The hello interval determines the frequency of a hello packet exchange to confirm the peer is alive. The default hello-interval is 1 second. The no form of this command sets the hello-interval for keepalive protocol to the default of 1 second. 118. Syntax: keepalive dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the keepalive dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: keepalive dead-interval <DEAD-INTERVAL>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the keepalive peer <PEER-IP-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The keepalive peer <PEER-IP-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command sets the source and peer IP addresses for keepalive packets in a specified VRF. If a VRF is not specified, it sets to the default VRF. The no form of this command removes the source and peer IP addresses and VRF for the keepalive protocol. VSX continues to work. Syntax: keepalive peer <PEER-IP-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the keepalive peer <PEER-IP-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: keepalive peer <PEER-IP-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the keepalive udp-port <PORT-NUM> command do?

The keepalive udp-port <PORT-NUM> command sets the UDP port for the keepalive protocol. The no form of this command sets the UDP port for keepalive protocol to the default of 7678. Syntax: keepalive udp-port <PORT-NUM>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the keepalive udp-port <PORT-NUM> command?
Syntax: keepalive udp-port <PORT-NUM>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lacp fallback command do?

The lacp fallback command sets LACP fallback on a VSX LAG port. When no LACP partner is detected, the VSX LAG port makes members of the VSX LAG function as nonbonded interfaces. To create a VSX LAG, use the interface lag multi- chassis command. The no form of this command sets the VSX LAG to a block state when no LACP partner is detected. Syntax: lacp fallback.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lacp fallback command?
Syntax: lacp fallback
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the linkup-delay-timer <DELAY-TIMER> command do?

The linkup-delay-timer <DELAY-TIMER> command configures the VSX link-up delay timer. The VSX delay timer feature lets you configure the delay timer, which delays bringing downstream VSX links up, following a VSX device reboot or an ISL flap. The no form of this command restores the VSX link-up delay timer to a default of 180 seconds. Configures the VSX link-up delay timer exclude list. It excludes the bringing up of specified downstream VSX LAGs, following a device reboot or an ISL flap. The no form of this command unconfigures the VSX link-up delay timer exclude list. Syntax: linkup-delay-timer <DELAY-TIMER>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the linkup-delay-timer <DELAY-TIMER> command?
Syntax: linkup-delay-timer <DELAY-TIMER>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command excludes VSX sync for the BGP neighbor. Configures the VSX device role. The no form of this command removes the device role of the switch in VSX and causes the interswitch link to be out-of-sync. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command?
Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show active-gateway command do?
The show active-gateway command displays the gateway information configured on SVIs, such as:. Syntax: show active-gateway [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show active-gateway command?
Syntax: show active-gateway [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show active-gateway <IFNAME> command do?

The show active-gateway <IFNAME> command displays the gateway information per SVI, such as: • Active-Gateway IPv4 and its MAC address • Active-Gateway IPv6 and its MAC address. Syntax: show active-gateway <IFNAME> [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show active-gateway <IFNAME> command?
Syntax: show active-gateway <IFNAME> [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface <VLAN-NAME> [vsx-peer] command do?
The show interface <VLAN-NAME> [vsx-peer] command displays a virtual IPv4/IPv6 and MAC configured for active-active routing.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lacp interfaces multi-chassis command do?

The show lacp interfaces multi-chassis command shows all configured VSX remote interface details. The interface that has the ALFNCD status has been synced with the partner and is ready for flow distribution. Syntax: show lacp interfaces multi-chassis [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lacp interfaces multi-chassis command?
Syntax: show lacp interfaces multi-chassis [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config vsx command do?
The show running-config vsx command displays the configured VSX commands. Syntax: show running-config vsx.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config vsx command?
Syntax: show running-config vsx
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config vsx-sync command do?

The show running-config vsx-sync command displays the lines of running-configuration that VSX configuration synchronization is enabled on. The command also provides a rolled-up view of configuration expected to be synced. This command can be run from the primary or secondary peer. 134. Syntax: show running-config vsx-sync.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config vsx-sync command?
Syntax: show running-config vsx-sync
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config vsx-sync peer-diff command do?

The show running-config vsx-sync peer-diff command displays the difference between the configuration of features enabled for VSX synchronization on the primary and secondary switches. Syntax: show running-config vsx-sync peer-diff.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config vsx-sync peer-diff command?
Syntax: show running-config vsx-sync peer-diff
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx active-forwarding [interface <INTERFACE-VLAN>] [vsx-peer] command do?

The show vsx active-forwarding [interface <INTERFACE-VLAN>] [vsx-peer] command shows all the VSX active-forwarding configured interface VLANs or the VSX active-forwarding peer information for a particular interface VLAN.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx config-consistency command do?
The show vsx config-consistency command displays the VSX global configuration consistency between two VSX switches. Syntax: show vsx config-consistency [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx config-consistency command?
Syntax: show vsx config-consistency [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx config-consistency lacp command do?
The show vsx config-consistency lacp command displays VSX LACP configuration consistency between two VSX switches. Syntax: show vsx config-consistency lacp [<LAG-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx config-consistency lacp command?
Syntax: show vsx config-consistency lacp [<LAG-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx ip data-path command do?

The show vsx ip data-path command displays the datapath of the IPv4 route present on local and VSX peer devices. Syntax: show vsx ip data-path [<IP-ADDR> | <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>] [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx ip data-path command?
Syntax: show vsx ip data-path [<IP-ADDR> | <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>] [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx ip route command do?
The show vsx ip route command displays a specified LAG or all configured LAGs along with VSX LAGs. Syntax: show vsx ip route [<IP-ADDR> | <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> | unique].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx ip route command?
Syntax: show vsx ip route [<IP-ADDR> | <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> | unique]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx IPv6 data-path command do?

The show vsx IPv6 data-path command displays the datapath of the IPv6 route on local and peer VSX devices. Syntax: show vsx IPv6 data-path [<IPv6-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>] [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx IPv6 data-path command?
Syntax: show vsx IPv6 data-path [<IPv6-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>] [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx IPv6 route command do?
The show vsx IPv6 route command displays a specified LAG or all configured LAGs along with VSX LAGs. Syntax: show vsx IPv6 route [<IPv6-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> | unique].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx IPv6 route command?
Syntax: show vsx IPv6 route [<IPv6-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> | unique]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx status command do?
The show vsx status command displays global VSX status or a specified status determined by the selected parameter. Syntax: show vsx status [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx status command?
Syntax: show vsx status [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vsx status config-sync command do?

The show vsx status config-sync command displays VSX configuration synchronization status for peers. This command can be run from the primary or secondary peer to view the configuration synchronization state. Syntax: show vsx status config-sync [vsx-peer].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vsx status config-sync command?
Syntax: show vsx status config-sync [vsx-peer]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the system-mac <MAC-ADDR> command do?

The system-mac <MAC-ADDR> command enables split recovery mode. Split recovery mode is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables split-recovery mode. Sets the MAC address as the VSX system MAC address to be used by control plane protocols, such as STP and LACP. A pair of VSX switches must have the same VSX system MAC. The no form of this command unconfigures the VSX system MAC address to be used by control plane protocols. Syntax: system-mac <MAC-ADDR>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the system-mac <MAC-ADDR> command?
Syntax: system-mac <MAC-ADDR>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dcb-global (8325 and 8360 series switches command do?
The vsx-sync dcb-global (8325 and 8360 series switches command is documented in this guide. Syntax: vsx-sync dcb-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dcb-global (8325 and 8360 series switches command?
Syntax: vsx-sync dcb-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync vsx-global command do?

The vsx-sync vsx-global command enables VSX synchronization of global VSX configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of global VSX configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing VSX feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: vsx-sync vsx-global.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync vsx-global command?
Syntax: vsx-sync vsx-global
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx update-software command do?
The vsx update-software command this command lets you update the software. Syntax: vsx update-software <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx update-software command?
Syntax: vsx update-software <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx update-software boot-bank command do?

The vsx update-software boot-bank command upgrades the VSX pairs using the specified bank on both the devices. This command compares whether the image versions are same in both the primary and secondary switches and reboots them in sequence, the VSX secondary switch followed by VSX primary switch. NOTE: Before executing this command, download the software image and install in the required boot banks. Syntax: vsx update-software boot-bank {primary | secondary}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx update-software boot-bank command?
Syntax: vsx update-software boot-bank {primary | secondary}

section: Label b: Then, the secondary VSX

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Label b: Then, the secondary VSX?

Label b: Then, the secondary VSX: switch brings down the VSX LAG member ports. Active gateway is unreachable Symptom You are unable to ping the active gateway. Action 1. Verify that kernel interface is created for active gateway: 003000000000001@vlan3: <NO-CARRIER, BROADCAST, UP, M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state LOWERLAYERDOWN group default qlen 1000 link/ether 00: 00: 00: 00: 00: 01 brd ff: ff: ff: ff: ff: ff 2. Verify that the active gateway IP is programmed correctly on kernel interface: 003000000000001@vlan3: <NO-CARRIER, BROADCAST, UP, M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state LOWERLAYERDOWN group default qlen 1000 link/ether 00: 00: 00: 00: 00: 01 brd ff: ff: ff: ff: ff: ff inet 10.0.0.3/32 scope global 003000000000001 valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever inet 20.0.0.3/32 scope global 003000000000001 valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever 3. Verify that the active gateway VMAC is correctly configured in the hardware. The command used is platform-specific. Refer to the hardware documentation for your switch. The following example from the 8400 series switch: 8400X: /home/admin# ovs-appctl l3pd/show router-mac -a 196.

topic: Label b: Then, the secondary VSX

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn evi command do?
The show evpn evi command shows the information of EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi command?
Syntax: show evpn evi
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn evi summary command do?
The show evpn evi summary command shows the summary information for EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi summary.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi summary command?
Syntax: show evpn evi summary
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> command do?
The show evpn evi <EVI-ID> command shows the information for the particular EVPN instance. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> command?
Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn evi detail command do?
The show evpn evi detail command shows the detailed information for all EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi detail.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi detail command?
Syntax: show evpn evi detail
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command do?
The show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command shows the detailed information for the particular EVPN instance. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command?
Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn mac-ip command do?
The show evpn mac-ip command show the information about the EVPN MAC-IP for the EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn mac-ip.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn mac-ip command?
Syntax: show evpn mac-ip
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn mac-ip evi command do?
The show evpn mac-ip evi command show the information about the EVPN MAC-IP for the particular EVPN instance. Syntax: show evpn mac-ip evi <EVI-ID>.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn mac-ip evi command?
Syntax: show evpn mac-ip evi <EVI-ID>
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show evpn vtep-neighbor command do?

The show evpn vtep-neighbor command shows the remote VTEPs MAC-IP binding. The state of the peer VTEP denotes whether VXLAN tunnel to the VTEP is Up or Down. Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show evpn vtep-neighbor command?
Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config evpn command?
Syntax: show running-config evpn
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Enables a layer 3 VNI for EVPN VXLAN distributed L3 gateways with symmetric IRB. The VNI is automatically assigned the default VRF. To assign another VRF, use the command vrf. Used with EVPN-based VXLAN s. The no form of this command disables symmetric routing on a VNI. Syntax: interface vxlan 1.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface vxlan command?
Syntax: interface vxlan 1
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface vxlan1 command do?
The show interface vxlan1 command shows detailed information about VXLAN interface 1. Syntax: show interface vxlan1.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan1 command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan1
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface vxlan vni command do?
The show interface vxlan vni command shows detailed VNI information for VXLAN interface 1. Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-NUMBER> vteps.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan vni command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-NUMBER> vteps
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?
The show interface vxlan vteps command shows information about the VTEPs on VXLAN interface 1. Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IPv4-ADDR>].
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan vteps command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IPv4-ADDR>]
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables BFD support on an HSC. The no form of this command disables BFD support on an HSC. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
Syntax: bfd enable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd disable command do?

The bfd disable command disables BFD support on an HSC. Disables a hardware switch controller (HSC). Enables a hardware switch controller (HSC). The no form of this command disables an HSC. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
Syntax: bfd disable
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the hsc command do?

The hsc command creates a hardware switch controller (HSC) and changes to the config-hsc context for it. The switch uses the HSC to communicate with a remote controller (such as VMware NSX) to create an extended a layer 2 VXLAN network. The HSC runs an open-source OVSDB server. OVSDB is the Open vSwitch Database Management Protocol detailed in RFC 7047. The remote controller connects to the server as an OVSDB client, and is responsible for managing the interaction with the HSC. As a result, the remote controller is known as the HSC manager. The no form of this command removes the HDC controller and deletes all configuration settings. 98 AOS-CX 10.06 VXLAN EVPN Guide. Syntax: hsc.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the hsc command?
Syntax: hsc
